title: Living With a Dell Latitude 9420 2-in-1
summary: Michael J. Miller reviews the Dell Latitude 9420 2-in-1, a premium enterprise convertible laptop designed for demanding customers. He details its features, performance, battery life, and Dell Optimizer software, noting its solid build and strong performance despite a few minor reservations. The article concludes that it is one of the best 2-in-1 laptops he has seen.

title: AMD Unveils New Ryzen Mobile Processors Uniting “Zen 3+” core with AMD RDNA 2 Graphics in Powerhouse Design
summary: AMD has announced its new Ryzen 6000 Series processors for laptops, combining the "Zen 3+" core architecture with RDNA 2 graphics for enhanced performance and battery life. Additionally, AMD introduced the Ryzen 7 5800X3D desktop processor with 3D V-Cache technology for superior gaming and preview previewed the upcoming Ryzen 7000 Series with "Zen 4" architecture. These announcements highlight AMD's focus on innovation across mobile and desktop computing, emphasizing efficiency, graphics capabilities, and security features.

title: Semiconductor giant Micron scouts Central Texas for major factory, sources say
summary: Micron Technology Inc. is considering Central Texas for a massive fabrication facility as part of its plan to invest over $150 billion in expanding its chipmaking capabilities over the next decade. While no final decision has been made, this investment could reportedly reach $40 billion in the region. Other states are also under consideration for the factory.

title: Miro Raises $400M in Series C Funding Round to Accelerate Innovation Through Visual Collaboration in the New Hybrid Workplace
summary: Miro, an online platform for visual collaboration, has closed a $400M Series C funding round, bringing its total funding to $476M and valuing the company at $17.5B. This investment will fuel product development and global expansion as Miro aims to further support organizations in unlocking creativity and strengthening collaboration in the new hybrid workplace. The company has seen significant growth since its Series B round in April 2020, increasing its user base to 30M and working with 99% of Fortune 100 companies.

title: New York developer closes on Mesa land, plans $200M industrial park funded by Dell Technologies CEO
summary: New York-based developer Wharton Industrial has acquired a 101-acre parcel in Mesa to build a $200 million industrial park. The project, named Hub@202, is situated near Phoenix-Mesa Gateway Airport and will be funded in part by Dell Technologies CEO Michael Dell's MSD Partners. The development aims to cater to the growing demand for industrial space in the region.

title: The top 20 companies on a hiring spree for remote workers this year
summary: FlexJobs released its annual report identifying the top 100 companies hiring for remote positions in 2022, with a particular focus on the top 20. The report highlights a significant increase in remote job listings and the continued demand for flexible work arrangements, driven by job seekers' preferences during the "Great Resignation." Companies like HubSpot, Mayo Clinic, and Stitch Fix are new to the list, while others like Anthem and Dell Technologies are consistent top employers for remote roles.

title: What will be included in 5G Advanced Release 18?
summary: The upcoming 5G Advanced Release 18 will focus on end-to-end system enhancements and expanding support for new device types and use cases. Qualcomm's Juan Montojo detailed improvements such as MIMO radio arrays, mobile Integrated Access/Backhaul, AI/ML-driven network design, enhanced mobility, and optimizing power consumption. The release also aims to develop a scalable, unified air interface to support a diverse range of devices, from high-performance XR headsets to various IoT sensors, paving the way for the future 6G vision.

title: How Intel relies on inclusion in retention and hiring—for big results
summary: Intel has been a leader in diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI) for over a decade, achieving full representation of women and underrepresented minorities in its U.S. workforce and global gender pay equity ahead of schedule. Dawn Jones, Intel's chief diversity and inclusion officer, discussed the company's continuous commitment to fostering an inclusive culture, especially amid the Great Resignation, and its efforts to track DEI progress through data and partnerships like the Alliance for Global Inclusion. Intel focuses on retention, progression, and recruitment, utilizing data-driven decision-making and a hybrid work model to support its diverse workforce globally.

title: Vodafone switches on first 5G OpenRAN site
summary: Vodafone has launched the UK's first 5G OpenRAN site in Bath, marking the initial step towards its commitment to deploy 2,500 5G and 4G OpenRAN sites by 2027. This initiative supports the government's strategy to diversify the telecoms supply chain and accelerate OpenRAN development. The technology, which separates software from hardware, is expected to foster innovation, improve energy efficiency, and speed up network deployment.

title: Top cloud security standards and frameworks to consider
summary: This article identifies key cloud security standards, frameworks, and organizations that help secure cloud systems and maintain data privacy. It lists professional and technical bodies like DMTF, ETSI, and OASIS, as well as standards organizations such as NIST and ISO, which provide guidelines and specifications for cloud security. The article also offers advice on selecting appropriate standards and preparing for cloud security audits.

title: Lenovo Launches HPC As-A-Service To Fight Dell Apex, HPE GreenLake
summary: Lenovo has launched a new High-Performance Computing (HPC) as-a-service offering as part of its TruScale portfolio, directly competing with Dell Technologies' Apex and HPE GreenLake. This service provides on-premises supercomputing capabilities with a pay-per-use model, managed by Lenovo and its channel partners. The move aligns with Lenovo's "One Lenovo" vision to expand beyond PCs into a comprehensive IT solutions provider and aims to reach $100 billion in revenue by taking market share from rivals.

title: Wayfinding tools chart course for hybrid office
summary: Wayfinding tools, including apps, maps, and electronic signage, are emerging as essential for navigating hybrid offices, where employees may need to reserve desks, locate colleagues, and find meeting spaces. Companies like ServiceNow are integrating these technologies into their platforms, although real-time employee tracking components raise privacy concerns. While beneficial for orientation and space utilization analysis, some experts argue against the necessity and cost of complex wireless wayfinding systems, suggesting simpler solutions may suffice.

title: Netgear Orbi Pro Wi-Fi 6 review: Super-fast and super pricey
summary: The Netgear Orbi Pro Wi-Fi 6 offers excellent Wi-Fi 6 performance, good range, and easy meshing for small businesses, but comes with a high price tag. While it includes valuable features like a 5-year Insight Premium subscription and robust security, its cost may deter smaller organizations that could find more affordable alternatives like the Netgear WAX630.

title: Apple, Broadcom win new trial in $1.1 bln Caltech patent case
summary: A U.S. appeals court has invalidated a $1.1 billion jury verdict against Apple and Broadcom in a patent infringement case brought by the California Institute of Technology (Caltech). The court deemed the damages "legally unsupportable" and ordered a new trial on the amount of damages, while upholding the infringement findings for two of Caltech's patents. The case revolves around Caltech's Wi-Fi technology patents used in millions of Apple devices containing Broadcom chips.

title: Apple, Broadcom Win New Trial In $1.1BN Caltech Patent Case
summary: A United States appeals court has overturned a jury's decision that ordered Apple and Broadcom to pay $1.1 billion to Caltech for infringing on Wi-Fi technology patents, ordering a new trial on damages. The court deemed the original award "legally unsupportable" but upheld the finding of infringement on two patents and called for a new trial on a third. Caltech's two-tier damages model was rejected by the appeals court, which found it legally unsound.

title: HP DeskJet Ink Advantage Ultra 4826 review: A no-nonsense printer that effectively helps your WFH producti
summary: This review covers the HP DeskJet Ink Advantage Ultra 4826 printer, highlighting its suitability for home and small office use, especially for those working from home. Priced at Rs. 10,279, it comes with two full cartridge sets capable of printing up to 2,600 mono and 1,400 color pages. The article praises its effective functionality, scanning capabilities, and compatibility with various operating systems, despite occasional Wi-Fi connectivity issues.

title: Xerox Acquires Powerland, Expanding IT Services Reach Across Canada
summary: Xerox Holdings Corp. has acquired Canadian IT services company Powerland in an all-cash deal to strengthen its IT services offerings in North America, with the aim of becoming a single provider for SMBs' IT and print needs across the region. Powerland, founded in 1985, will retain its name with the addition of "A Xerox Business Solutions Company" and its approximately 100 employees will join Xerox. This acquisition is part of Xerox's strategy to expand its IT services business, following other recent acquisitions in the space.

title: NetApp: Storage Management Portfolio Review
summary: This article provides a comprehensive review of NetApp's storage management portfolio, detailing its various offerings like SolidFire, FAS, ONTAP, and Azure NetApp File. It highlights NetApp's market position, financial performance, partner programs, and customer use cases, noting its significant presence in the external enterprise storage systems and all-flash array markets. The review also includes user feedback and industry recognition for NetApp's solutions.

title: TechTarget’s Storage Magazine and SearchStorage.com Announce “Storage Products of the Year” 2021 Award Winners
summary: TechTarget’s Storage Magazine and SearchStorage.com have announced the winners of their 20th annual "Storage Products of the Year" awards for 2021. The awards recognize top data storage products across five categories, including Backup and Disaster Recovery, Cloud Storage, and Disk and Disk Subsystems. An independent team of experts and editors evaluated products based on innovation, value, performance, functionality, and ease of use.

title: Build your knowledge of Azure AD conditional access policies
summary: This article explains how Azure Active Directory (Azure AD) conditional access policies help IT control access to resources in a dynamic, cloud-heavy environment. It details how these policies use signals and real-time risk intelligence to grant, restrict, or deny access, building a layered security approach. The article also covers the configuration of conditional access policies, including setting conditions based on user risk, sign-in risk, device platforms, and location, and how to filter access to privileged resources.

title: Samsung Joins Forces with Industry Leaders to Advance 5G vRAN Ecosystem
summary: Samsung has partnered with Dell, HPE, Intel, Red Hat, and Wind River to advance the 5G virtualized RAN (vRAN) ecosystem. This collaboration aims to drive innovation and multiparty efforts for more scalable, flexible, and reliable software-based networks. Samsung, a leader in fully-virtualized 5G RAN, seeks to move vRAN and Open RAN beyond lab tests to major commercial markets with this new ecosystem.

title: With Systems, HPE Walks The Line Between Demand And Supply
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) is navigating a challenging economic environment by focusing on its core enterprise business and transitioning to its GreenLake cloud utility pricing model. Despite supply chain issues and slow revenue growth in some divisions, HPE is seeing strong order growth, particularly in its "as-a-service" offerings, and is building a substantial revenue backlog. The company aims for significant compound annual growth in its GreenLake-driven annualized revenue run rate by fiscal 2024.

title: Nvidia invests USD 10 million in Serve Robotics
summary: Nvidia has invested $10 million in Serve Robotics, a company specializing in sidewalk delivery robots. This investment follows Serve Robotics' expanded seed round in December 2021 and will help the startup broaden its services beyond Los Angeles and San Francisco. Serve's robots utilize Nvidia's Jetson edge AI platform for autonomous movement and will integrate Nvidia's perception and mapping tools, alongside synthetic data generation for testing.

title: Expedia Group CPO on risk management & strategic sourcing
summary: Reagan Smith, Head of Global Corporate Procurement at Expedia Group, discusses his journey into procurement, his biggest career influences, and his passion for elevating the indirect procurement function. He emphasizes continuous learning, challenging established practices, and aligning procurement strategies with business needs for success. Smith also shares personal anecdotes, including his pride in advocating for the LGBTQ community.

title: Baker Hughes joins Halliburton and Schlumberger in pausing Russian operations
summary: Baker Hughes announced it is suspending new investments for its Russia operations, following similar moves by rivals Halliburton Co. and Schlumberger, in response to U.S. sanctions over Russia’s invasion of Ukraine. This decision means these major oil field services companies are winding down or suspending activities in Russia, joining over 400 other multinational firms. Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has urged remaining U.S. businesses to leave Russia, stating the market is "flooded with our blood."

title: 3rd Gen AMD EPYC Processors with AMD 3D V-Cache Technology Deliver Outstanding Leadership Performance in Technical Computing Workloads
summary: AMD has announced the general availability of its 3rd Gen EPYC processors with AMD 3D V-Cache technology, codenamed "Milan-X." These processors are the world's first data center CPUs to use 3D die stacking and are built on the "Zen 3" core architecture. They offer up to a 66% performance uplift in technical computing workloads and feature the industry's largest L3 cache for applications like CFD, FEA, and EDA.

title: NetApp Cloud Insights gets AI monitoring and ransomware detection
summary: NetApp has enhanced its Cloud Insights infrastructure management software by integrating AI/machine learning capabilities. This upgrade enables automatic generation of IT infrastructure topologies, prediction of event impacts, and the detection of anomalous behavior to combat ransomware. The software also suggests solutions to administrators and can trigger data protection measures.

title: Nvidia debuts next-generation H100 GPUs, its most powerful chips yet
summary: Nvidia has unveiled its next-generation H100 GPUs, built on the new Hopper architecture, which promise an order of magnitude performance improvement over previous models. These chips feature 80 billion transistors, a Transformer Engine for accelerating AI language models, and HBM3 memory. The H100 GPUs are designed for advanced AI workloads, including real-time inference, and will be available to customers and cloud providers in the third quarter.

title: 10 Servers With AMD EPYC Milan-X New Server CPUs
summary: AMD has launched its third-generation EPYC processors with 3-D V-Cache technology, code-named Milan-X, which is the world's first data center CPU using 3-D die stacking. This innovation allows for 768 MB of L3 cache, significantly enhancing performance for technical computing workloads like FEA, EDA, and CFD. Major server vendors such as Dell Technologies, HPE, Cisco, Lenovo, and Supermicro are rolling out new or upgraded servers supporting these processors, aiming to offer improved performance, lower total cost of ownership, and reduced power consumption.

title: NetApp is increasing targets thanks to CloudOps biz
summary: NetApp has raised its revenue targets due to the strong performance and growth prospects of its Cloud Operations (CloudOps) business. The company expects its public cloud annual recurring revenue (ARR) to reach $2 billion by fiscal year 2026, accelerating its $1 billion target by a year. NetApp's unique CloudOps portfolio, which helps customers manage and optimize applications in the public cloud, is seen as a key differentiator and a significant opportunity for expanding its customer base and cross-selling.

title: Driving digital customer onboarding success.
summary: GP Bullhound advised HooYu on its £98M acquisition by Mitek Systems, Inc. HooYu, a fast-growing Know Your Customer (KYC) platform, specializes in digital customer onboarding and serves highly regulated industries. This acquisition is seen as a strategic move to combine HooYu's platform with Mitek's computer vision and biometrics technology to create a leading global onboarding solution.

title: Intel Becomes Latest Western Tech Firm to Suspend Business in Russia
summary: US chipmaker Intel Corp has announced the suspension of its business operations in Russia, joining other Western companies exiting the country following the invasion of Ukraine. This move follows Intel's earlier decision to halt shipments to Russia and Belarus, as the company states it is implementing business continuity measures and condemns Russia's actions. Other major tech firms like IBM have also suspended shipments, as Ukraine urged US cloud-computing and software companies to cut ties with Russia.

title: Retail, Tech, and Innovation Leaders Join North Texas AR Startup’s New Advisory Board
summary: Spacee, a North Texas-based augmented reality company specializing in computer vision and AI, has appointed a three-person advisory board comprising Brian Crutcher, Jeff Wellen, and Duncan MacFarlane. CEO Skip Howard emphasized the timing as "critical" due to the return of in-store shopping and the need for interactive consumer experiences that provide e-commerce-level data. The new board members bring expertise in technology, retail, and innovation, areas crucial for Spacee's continued product development in AI and computer vision.

title: Leveraging Intel Technology as a Force for Good
summary: Intel's RISE Technology Initiative supported 63 projects in the past year, investing $9.5 million to leverage Intel technology, employee talent, and partnerships for global good. These projects address accessibility, economic recovery, education, health and life sciences, social equity and human rights, and sustainability and climate. The initiative demonstrates how technology can be a force for positive change across diverse communities worldwide.

title: Why did Buffett buy HP Inc (and should you invest too)?
summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway recently acquired an 11.4% stake in HP Inc., making it Berkshire's second-largest tech position after Apple. This investment comes amidst concerns about inflation and the PC market, yet HP's strong fundamentals, consistent cash flow, strategic expansion into gaming and remote work solutions, and attractive valuation at a low P/E ratio and a 2.6% dividend yield, make it an appealing "boring but dependable cash machine" for value investors. The article questions whether individual investors should follow Buffett, advising personal research and considering pullbacks before investing.

title: Apple, others face shipment delays as China COVID curbs squeeze suppliers
summary: Shipments of Apple products, Dell, and Lenovo laptops are facing potential delays due to China's ongoing COVID-19 lockdowns, which have forced suppliers like Pegatron and Quanta Computer to suspend operations. The impact's severity depends on the lockdown duration, with a longer lockdown potentially causing significant shortages for end-users, despite some companies' efforts to reroute production or use alternative plants.

title: TRAVEL TECHNOLOGY: The Dell 5420 – Cheapness over Mediocrity?
summary: This review offers a hands-on look at the Dell Latitude 5420 business laptop, evaluating its design, features, performance, and overall value. While praising its performance and battery life improvements over previous models, the author criticizes the build quality, particularly the "cheap" feel of the keyboard and trackpad. The article concludes that despite adequate performance, the Dell 5420's build quality issues make it difficult to recommend for individual buyers, although corporate purchases might continue due to other factors.

title: HP Spectre x360 14 Vs. Dell XPS 13: 2-in-1 Laptops Face Off
summary: This article compares the HP Spectre x360 14 and Dell XPS 13, two prominent 2-in-1 laptops. It evaluates them across several categories including dimensions, display, ports, memory, and performance specifications. The HP Spectre x360 14 is ultimately declared the winner due to its superior display, specs, and features, despite its higher price point.

title: Oracle Database 19c on Cisco X210c M6 – WOW!
summary: This article details the performance testing of Oracle Database 19c on Cisco X210c M6 compute nodes. Using Oracle SLOB and SwingBench benchmarks, it demonstrates excellent scalability, low latency, and high transaction rates, highlighting the suitability of the Cisco UCS X210c M6 for hosting single-instance Oracle databases. The article also addresses performance impact during disk failure, showing minimal disruption and efficient recovery through Oracle ASM.

title: Israel's Emza providing visual sensing tech for new Dell laptops
summary: Israel's Emza Visual Sense is integrating its WiseEye technology into new Dell Latitude, Inspiron, and Precision laptops. This AI-based visual sensing system uses ultra-low-power algorithms to detect user engagement, enhancing display power management and battery life. Key features include automatic screen dimming when a user looks away, blurring the screen for on-looker detection, and reliable presence detection for faster sign-in and screen locking.

title: Embracing The Inevitable Move From Hardware To Services
summary: Dell Technologies is shifting its focus from hardware sales to software and services, as evidenced by its Dell Technologies World event. The company is expanding its Apex as-a-service portfolio, enhancing its storage software, and building multi-cloud solutions like Project Alpine to offer cloud-like consumption models and address the evolving needs of IT. This strategy aims to provide streamlined experiences, consistent data mobility, and robust cybersecurity solutions for on-premises and public cloud environments.

title: Dell cuts data center redundancy for SMC Corporation
summary: SMC Corporation is consolidating its global IT infrastructure, moving from 85 disparate IT departments to 12 centralized data centers with the help of Dell Technologies. This initiative, led by Jon St. Arnaud, aims to create a private hybrid cloud using Dell's VxRail hyper-converged systems, PowerScale storage, and Ransomware Defender security tools. The consolidation is expected to improve collaboration among engineering teams, enhance data protection, and lead to significant cost savings by reducing redundant software purchases.

title: HP debuts 2 new Chromebooks for business in the Elite c600 series with AMD and Intel on board
summary: HP has introduced two new Chromebooks, the Elite c640 G3 and Elite c645 G2, aimed at business users but also available to consumers. These devices emphasize security and productivity for hybrid work environments, with the c645 G2 featuring AMD Ryzen 5000 C-Series processors and the c640 G3 utilizing Intel's 12th-gen processors, along with enhanced features like improved screens, advanced security options, and AI capabilities for audio and visual. Both models are designed to offer a robust and affordable option within HP's high-end Chromebook portfolio, starting in the $500 range.

title: China Orders Government, State Firms to Dump Foreign PCs
summary: China has mandated that central government agencies and state-backed corporations replace foreign-branded personal computers with domestic alternatives within two years. This move aims to eliminate key overseas technology from sensitive government operations and is expected to replace at least 50 million PCs. Staff were instructed to swap foreign PCs for local ones running domestically developed operating software after the May holiday.

title: How Elliott Associates' proposal to split two businesses at Western Digital may build value
summary: Elliott Associates has proposed that Western Digital separate its NAND flash memory business from its hard disk drive (HDD) business, believing such a move could significantly increase shareholder value, potentially leading to a stock price exceeding $100 per share by the end of 2023. Western Digital, formed by the 2016 acquisition of SanDisk, has seen underperformance, and Elliott argues that the two technically distinct businesses would thrive as standalones. Elliott is demonstrating its commitment by offering $1+ billion in incremental equity capital for the Flash business, indicating strong conviction in its valuation.

title: Western Digital's New Purpose-Built SSD Unlocks the Potential of Data for Today's Hybrid Workforce
summary: Western Digital introduced the PC SN740 NVMe SSD, designed to meet the demands of today's hybrid workforce by offering enhanced performance, security, and power efficiency. This new SSD features PCIe Gen4 technology, sequential read speeds up to 5,150 MB/s, and advanced security options, making it suitable for modern PC OEMs. It is built with Western Digital's in-house SSD controller design, BiCS5 3D NAND, and firmware development, emphasizing the company's vertically integrated approach to innovation.

title: Intel Announces New Cloud-to-Edge Technologies to Solve Challenges of Today and Tomorrow
summary: At its inaugural Intel Vision event, Intel announced significant advancements across silicon, software, and services designed to address complex challenges and accelerate digital transformations for customers. Key announcements include new AI processors like Habana Gaudi2, 12th Gen Intel Core HX processors for hybrid work, and updates on 4th Gen Intel Xeon Scalable processors, alongside initiatives like Project Apollo for enterprise AI, the IPU roadmap, and Project Endgame for on-demand computing. The company emphasized real-world applications in industries such as education, finance, manufacturing, and medicine, highlighting collaborations with partners to deliver impactful technological solutions.

title: ScanSource CEO: MSPs Becoming A ‘Source Of Growth’ For The Distributor
summary: ScanSource's top executives discussed their hybrid distribution strategy as a key driver for growth, helping partners connect devices to the cloud. CEO Mike Baur noted that MSPs are a growing part of their business, particularly in the U.K. and Brazil, and are becoming a significant source of growth in the U.S. as ScanSource expands its recurring revenue and SaaS capabilities. The company reported strong financial results for its fiscal third quarter 2022, with total revenue up 15.9 percent.

title: Cohesity protects user data with FortKnox vault service
summary: Cohesity has launched FortKnox, a managed SaaS data protection service that provides an air-gapped digital vault within AWS. This service aims to enhance cyber resilience and recovery times for organizations against ransomware attacks and other disaster recovery concerns. It allows users to create an isolated backup copy of their data, addressing a critical need for robust data protection without requiring extensive in-house expertise.

title: Pure Storage, Snowflake partner on analyzing local data
summary: Pure Storage and Snowflake have partnered to enable data analytics on Pure's on-premises FlashBlade storage using Snowflake's Data Cloud capabilities. This collaboration allows customers to analyze large local datasets without moving them to the cloud, addressing data control and migration challenges. The partnership leverages Amazon S3 and aims to provide hybrid cloud advantages for analytical workloads, with the public preview expected later this year.

title: Management shakeup at Arrow Electronics ...
summary: Arrow Electronics is undergoing a significant management restructuring in its US and European operations with a new CEO, Sean Kerins, taking over. Kirk Schell will lead the global components business, succeeding David West. Jörg Strughold has been appointed president of the EMEA components business, replacing Martin Bielesch, as the company aims to build on growth in its components sector.

title: Apple introduces new professional training to support growing IT workforce
summary: Apple has launched updated professional training and certifications for IT support and management, delivered through new online, self-paced courses. These certifications, including Apple Certified Support Professional and Apple Certified IT Professional, aim to address the growing demand for IT professionals skilled in supporting Apple products within enterprises. Apple is also partnering with community colleges, universities, and the Mac Admins Foundation to provide scholarships and broad access to these new training programs.

title: Broadcom in talks to buy VMware
summary: Chipmaker Broadcom Inc. is reportedly in talks to acquire cloud service provider VMware Inc., according to sources familiar with the matter. This acquisition would further Broadcom's diversification into enterprise software, following previous purchases of CA Technologies and Symantec's security division. VMware's market capitalization was $40.3 billion as of Friday's close, with Michael Dell holding a 40% stake and Silver Lake owning 10%.

title: Broadcom in Talks to Acquire VMware, the Cloud Computing Company (Published 2022)
summary: Broadcom, a semiconductor giant, is reportedly in discussions to acquire VMware, a cloud computing company known for its virtualization software. This potential acquisition would expand Broadcom's presence in cloud computing and continue CEO Hock E. Tan's strategy of diversifying beyond chip companies following a blocked deal with Qualcomm in 2018. Following the news, VMware's stock saw a significant increase, while Broadcom's shares experienced a slight dip.

title: Chipmaker Broadcom in talks to acquire VMware for $60 billion -sources
summary: Broadcom Inc is reportedly in talks to acquire cloud service provider VMware Inc for $60 billion, a move that would significantly expand Broadcom's enterprise software business. The proposed deal, valued at around $140 per share in cash and stock for VMware, represents a 46% premium to VMware's Friday share price. This acquisition aligns with Broadcom CEO Hock Tan's strategy of diversifying beyond chips into infrastructure software.

title: Broadcom in talks to purchase cloud computing company VMware
summary: Chipmaker Broadcom is reportedly in advanced talks to acquire cloud computing company VMware in a cash-and-stock deal, aiming to further diversify its business into enterprise software. This potential acquisition follows Broadcom's previous purchases of CA Technologies and Symantec's security division. VMware's shares jumped on the news, while Broadcom's saw a slight drop, as details of the deal's value remain undisclosed.

title: ServiceNow bought a year's worth of data center hardware in case of supply chain issues
summary: ServiceNow purchased a year's supply of data center equipment in January 2022 to proactively mitigate potential supply chain disruptions. The company's CTO, Pat Casey, noted that pre-pandemic hardware delivery times of 45 days had ballooned to over 120-160 days due to shortages. By placing their entire 2022 order early, ServiceNow aimed to avoid significant delays that would push hardware delivery into the following year, highlighting its strategy for self-managed infrastructure.

title: Broadcom to Buy VMware for $61 Billion in Record Chip Deal
summary: Broadcom Inc. has agreed to acquire cloud-computing company VMware Inc. for approximately $61 billion, marking one of the largest technology deals in history. This acquisition will strengthen Broadcom's software portfolio, a key part of CEO Hock Tan's strategy to diversify the chipmaker and expand its presence in corporate software. The deal, supported by major VMware shareholders, includes commitments for $32 billion in debt financing and is expected to close in Broadcom’s fiscal year 2023.

title: Broadcom buys VMware in a $61B acquisition deal
summary: Broadcom has formally announced plans to acquire VMware in a cash and stock deal valued at approximately $61 billion, plus an additional $8 billion in VMware net debt. The deal is expected to close in Broadcom's 2023 financial year, pending regulatory and shareholder approvals. This acquisition will see Broadcom's existing infrastructure and security software integrated into an expanded VMware portfolio, with the Broadcom Software Group rebranding to operate as VMware.

title: How Broadcom CEO Tan shaped a tech giant through acquisitions
summary: Hock Tan, CEO of Broadcom, has transformed the company into a $225 billion technology conglomerate primarily through a series of strategic acquisitions. His latest move is the proposed $61 billion acquisition of VMware, which underscores his reputation as an astute dealmaker and cost-cutter. Tan's strategy involves acquiring "franchise" businesses, streamlining operations by cutting sales and marketing expenses, and divesting underperforming assets, all while rapidly paying down debt to enable further acquisitions.

title: Broadcom will tame the VMware beast
summary: Broadcom's planned acquisition of VMware for $61 billion signifies a new era for the virtualization leader, focusing on financial performance and shareholder value. The article details Broadcom's strategy of optimizing profitability by focusing on recurring revenue, cutting R&D and sales/marketing expenses for less profitable areas, and standardizing G&A. For customers, this likely means higher prices and a more focused product portfolio, with core offerings like vSphere being emphasized while less profitable or unproven ventures like Project Monterey face scrutiny.

title: Top Global Systems Makers Accelerate Adoption of NVIDIA Grace and Grace Hopper
summary: NVIDIA announced that top global computer makers including Atos, Dell Technologies, GIGABYTE, HPE, Inspur, Lenovo, and Supermicro are adopting its new Grace and Grace Hopper superchips to build next-generation servers for AI and HPC workloads. These systems, utilizing the NVIDIA HGX platform, aim to deliver high performance and energy efficiency. Leading supercomputing centers like Los Alamos National Laboratory and the Swiss National Computing Center are among the first to implement these superchips, with systems like Venado expected to exceed 10 exaflops of AI performance.

title: Hackers ransom 1,200 exposed Elasticsearch databases
summary: Threat actors have hijacked over 1,200 unsecured Elasticsearch databases, replacing data with ransom demands for bitcoin payments. The attackers leveraged automated scripts to find instances with open read/write operations, not exploiting vulnerabilities, but rather misconfigurations. Secureworks reports that while ransom demands totaled over $280,000, there's no indication any payments were made or that data was backed up, making restoration unlikely.

title: An Ode to PCMag: A Personal Computing Legacy
summary: Michael J. Miller, former editor-in-chief of PC Magazine, reflects on his 14 years leading the publication and its evolution over four decades. He highlights PC Labs' pioneering role in product testing, the magazine's impact on the direct PC market, and its adaptation from print to a fully online model while maintaining its mission of expert analysis and practical solutions.

title: Dell, VMWare Pay $43 Million to Exit Suit Over $2.7 Billion Deal
summary: Dell Technologies Inc. and its partial subsidiary VMWare Inc. have agreed to pay $42.5 million to settle investor litigation. The lawsuit alleged that Dell and Michael Dell unfairly engineered the $2.7 billion take-private buyout of Pivotal Software Inc., leveraging their control to transfer value from Pivotal to VMWare. The settlement resolves claims that the deal disproportionately benefited Dell and VMWare due to their differing economic stakes in the companies involved.

title: The Long Term Profitability Of HPE Depends On GreenLake
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) is navigating supply chain constraints and varied business unit performance, with its long-term profitability increasingly tied to the success of its GreenLake utility model. Despite some divisions facing challenges or operating losses, HPE posted decent Q2 FY22 results, driven by strong compute demand and record backlogs across its core businesses. The company is actively transitioning to a subscription-based, "as-a-Service" approach with GreenLake, aiming for higher margins through software and services, which it believes is a superior strategy compared to building its own competing cloud.

title: Broadcom-VMware Merger Agreement Stock/Cash Election Provides Tax Advantages to VMware Shareholders
summary: This article analyzes the tax implications of the Broadcom-VMware merger agreement, focusing on the stock/cash election mechanism for VMware shareholders. It explains how this structure, particularly the ability to specifically identify shares for either cash or stock, offers potential tax advantages for shareholders, especially those with varying basis levels like Michael Dell. The authors delve into the complex rules surrounding redemptions, boot, and dividend withholding, highlighting the parties' apparent intent to enable beneficial tax treatment distinct from standard boot rules.

title: WAN Connectivity Matters in Delivering Exceptional Application Experiences
summary: This article highlights the critical importance of WAN connectivity in delivering high-quality application experiences, especially with the shift to hybrid and multicloud architectures. It details the partnership between Cisco and Equinix, offering integrated solutions to optimize WAN architecture by addressing latency, security, compliance, and cost. The collaboration focuses on four core use cases: secure cloud connectivity, distributed WAN architecture, private cloud footprint at the edge, and collaboration services, aiming to improve user experience and operational efficiency.

title: HYCU funding to aid multi-cloud data protection, management
summary: HYCU Inc. secured a $53 million Series B funding round, bringing its total funding to over $140 million. This investment will enable the company to expand its multi-cloud backup and recovery product line, increase its workforce, and develop a new data protection platform. HYCU aims to grow its customer base significantly and continues to pursue plans for an initial public offering, positioning itself as "recession-proof" in the data protection market.

title: Deal Diary: Gibson, Wachtell Lead VMware Sale to Broadcom
summary: Gibson, Dunn & Crutcher LLP advised VMware Inc. on its $61 billion sale to Broadcom Inc., with Broadcom also assuming $8 billion in debt. Wachtell, Lipton, Rosen & Katz, O’Melveny & Myers LLP, and Cleary Gottlieb Steen & Hamilton LLP counseled Broadcom. Various financial institutions provided advice to both parties, and Silver Lake Partners, a 10% owner of VMware, was represented by Simpson Thacher & Bartlett LLP, while Michael Dell (42% owner) was advised by Wachtell Lipton.

title: Broadcom's acquire-and-axe history concerns VMware users
summary: VMware users and employees are expressing concern over Broadcom's acquisition of the company, fearing a repeat of Broadcom's historical strategy of acquiring and then divesting parts of companies. Broadcom's CEO, Hock Tan, intends to transition VMware to a subscription-based model and integrate Broadcom's infrastructure and security software. The acquisition timing also coincides with a trend where corporate IT shops are moving towards SaaS-based solutions, diminishing VMware's traditional market.

title: MinIO extends Snowflake analysis to any data location
summary: MinIO has partnered with Snowflake to enable Snowflake analytics on data stored in any location, not just within Snowflake's own environment. This partnership allows customers to leverage external table support in MinIO for cost-effective analysis of large datasets without moving them to the cloud. This provides flexibility similar to recent Snowflake partnerships with Dell and Pure Storage, offering various options for on-premises or cloud-based data analysis.

title: Report: EU plans to investigate Broadcom’s proposed acquisition of VMware
summary: The European Union is preparing to launch a lengthy "phase 2" antitrust investigation into Broadcom's proposed $61 billion acquisition of VMware. This investigation could last over a year and potentially derail the deal, as multiple organizations, including VMware customer trade groups, have expressed concerns about potential price increases or forced bundling of Broadcom services. Broadcom is reportedly in preliminary discussions with EU officials to argue that the acquisition will not reduce competition or harm customers.

title: cnvrg.io, an Intel Company, Wins AI Breakthrough Award for MLOps Platform of the Year
summary: cnvrg.io, an Intel company, has won the AI Breakthrough Award for "MLOps Platform of the Year," marking its second consecutive win from the organization. The company was recognized for its two recent developer-friendly AI innovations: cnvrg.io AI Blueprints and cnvrg.io Metacloud, which enable seamless management, building, and deployment of AI on any infrastructure. This award highlights cnvrg.io's cutting-edge technology and its commitment to streamlining the machine learning lifecycle for enterprises.

title: HPE chief Antonio Neri discusses as-a-service, GreenLake’s impact and the critical importance of silicon
summary: HPE CEO Antonio Neri celebrated the company's successful transition to an as-a-service model with its GreenLake platform, fulfilling a 2019 commitment. Neri highlighted GreenLake's growth, now managing over an exabyte of data for 65,000 customers, and emphasized the critical role of silicon in HPE's innovation, including its supercomputers and support for the CHIPS For America Act. He also touched upon the company's stance on societal issues, specifically supporting women's reproductive rights.

title: Data center market in Malaysia to observe USD 2.08 Bn growth: Report
summary: The data center market in Malaysia is projected to grow by USD 2.08 billion between 2021 and 2026, exhibiting a CAGR of 15.72%. According to a Technavio report, the market is expected to see 14.86% year-over-year growth in 2022, with data center servers anticipated to generate the most revenue. Key vendors like Cisco Systems Inc. and Huawei Technologies Co. Ltd. are focusing on M&A and R&D to stay competitive in this fragmented market.

title: Salesforce harnesses Dell PowerMax for mission-critical storage
summary: Salesforce Inc. leverages Dell Technologies Inc.'s PowerMax for its mission-critical storage needs, prioritizing comprehensive cyber resiliency, multicloud flexibility, and adaptable software architectures. Dell PowerMax offers advanced security features and superior performance compared to software-defined storage for large-scale applications, as highlighted by discussions at Dell Technologies World. This partnership emphasizes trust and a customer-first mentality from both companies, with Salesforce being the largest PowerMax customer globally.

title: EXCLUSIVE BC Partners explores $4 bln sale of IT firm Presidio -sources
summary: Private equity firm BC Partners Ltd is reportedly exploring the sale of Presidio Inc., a U.S. information technology provider, for over $4 billion, including debt. Presidio, which generated $3.1 billion in revenue in 2021, specializes in secured cloud-based technology for a wide range of organizations and has benefited from increased demand for IT services during the COVID-19 pandemic. This potential sale follows a history of private equity ownership, with BC Partners having taken the company private in 2019 for $2.1 billion.

title: Hock Tan: Broadcom’s Plan For VMware As Krause Departs
summary: Broadcom CEO Hock Tan addressed VMware employees after the unexpected departure of software leader Tom Krause, who was originally slated to lead VMware post-acquisition. Tan announced he would personally oversee the integration efforts, ensuring VMware becomes part of the Broadcom Software Group, which he will now run. This comes as Broadcom proceeds with its $61 billion acquisition of VMware, which is expected to close by the end of its fiscal year 2023.

title: SingleStore secures $116M in funding to fuel growth
summary: SingleStore, a data management vendor, has secured an additional $116 million in venture capital funding, bringing its total financing to $278 million since December 2020 and $382 million overall. The company plans to use these funds for continued product development, marketing expansion to increase market awareness, and potentially withstand a challenging economic climate. CEO Raj Verma aims for SingleStore to become a billion-dollar company within the next three to five years, emphasizing innovation and eventual public offering.

title: Taiwan edge AI company Gorilla Technology IPO surges 123% on first day of Nasdaq trading
summary: Taiwanese edge AI company Gorilla Technology Group saw its IPO surge 123% on its first day of Nasdaq trading, closing at US$33.43 under the ticker "GRRR." The company plans to use the proceeds from its business combination with Global SPAC Partners for working capital and international expansion, aiming to form a "Smart City Diverse Industry Alliance" to expand global markets for edge AI analytics solutions. Gorilla Technology has developed its edge AI technology over 20 years, holding 31 patents, and provides comprehensive video-centric and content management solutions across various sectors with key partnerships including Softbank, Intel, and Dell.

title: Reddit Turns One Down Under
summary: Reddit is celebrating its one-year anniversary of expanding into Australia, its fourth-largest user base, by launching its first national consumer brand campaign, "Find your people." The campaign, developed with R/GA Australia and Initiative, highlights Reddit's diverse communities through out-of-home, digital, and audio advertising. Over the past year, Reddit has tripled its local team and fostered connections between global and Australian brands with its growing Australian audience.

title: Reddit Turns One Down Under
summary: Reddit is celebrating its one-year anniversary of expanding into Australia, which is now its fourth-largest user base. To commemorate this milestone, Reddit is launching its inaugural Australian consumer brand campaign, "Find your people," highlighting its diverse communities and increasing engagement with Australian users and brands. The platform has seen significant growth in local teams and user activity across various communities, including a successful partnership with the Australian Football League and effective ad campaigns for brands like Up.

title: Living With the Lenovo Yoga 9i Gen 7
summary: The article provides a detailed review of the Lenovo Yoga 9i Gen 7, a high-end 14-inch convertible laptop. It highlights standout features such as its sound bar built into the hinge, long battery life, and solid build quality, while also noting areas for improvement like the webcam's software and the lack of an integrated pen dock. The author, Michael J. Miller, shares his user experience over several weeks, evaluating its performance, design, and practical functionalities.

title: The modern data platform drives data-centric organizations
summary: The article discusses how modern data platforms are crucial for data-centric organizations, acting as conduits to deliver correct data to the right users for data-driven decision-making. It highlights the challenges posed by increasing data volumes, diverse data sources, and the growing demand for AI/machine learning tools, emphasizing the need for interoperable and scalable solutions rather than siloed approaches. The piece also touches on how IT's role is evolving to be more data-centric, with a focus on quick and clean data delivery to empower business objectives.

title: DISA creates new tech incubator, DISAWERX, to support innovation
summary: The Defense Information Systems Agency (DISA) has launched DISAWERX, a new technology incubator designed to help Pentagon personnel prototype and refine IT capabilities quickly. This initiative, inspired by the Air Force's AFWERX, aims to cut through bureaucratic hurdles and foster innovation through a virtual environment, event organizer, and physical lab in Maryland. DISAWERX provides a space for government, industry, and academia to experiment with emerging technologies, including opportunities for testing commercial and open-source solutions.

title: CACI Announces Partnership with the Alabama School of Cyber Engineering and Technology
summary: CACI International Inc has announced a partnership with the Alabama School of Cyber Engineering and Technology (ASCTE) Foundation. This collaboration aims to develop a robust talent pipeline for national security careers by offering internships, mentorships, curriculum development, and career coaching to ASCTE students. CACI's CEO, John Mengucci, highlighted the partnership's role in providing cutting-edge technology and resources while building a talent pipeline for government and industry.

title: Emerging approaches to HCI for IT services
summary: The article discusses how hyper-converged infrastructure (HCI) is evolving to meet the demands of modern data centers, particularly with increased support for containers, composability, and hybrid cloud deployments. It highlights HCI's benefits, such as reduced costs and improved efficiency, while also addressing drawbacks like resource underuse. The piece further explores disaggregated HCI (dHCI) and composable infrastructure as solutions for more flexible and scalable IT services.

title: Dell’s Weak Outlook Weighs On HP. And The Other HP.
summary: Dell's weak outlook is negatively impacting both HP Inc. and Hewlett Packard Enterprise, leading investors to sell off their stocks. Both companies are scheduled to report earnings on Tuesday afternoon and have been struggling with parts shortages.

title: National Cybersecurity Alliance Launches HBCU Career Program
summary: The National Cybersecurity Alliance (NCA) has launched a Historically Black Colleges and Universities (HBCU) Career Program to address the cybersecurity workforce gap and increase diversity in the industry. Collaborating with HBCUs and cybersecurity organizations like Dell Technologies and Proofpoint, the program will equip students with job search skills for security, privacy, and risk roles. Initiatives will include mentoring, networking events, and discussions on career paths and interviewing, aiming to build a pipeline of Black professionals.

title: VMware CEO weighs in on Broadcom acquisition, multicloud and why supercloud is the future
summary: VMware CEO Raghu Raghuram discussed the planned Broadcom acquisition, his company's multicloud strategy, and the evolution toward supercloud, emphasizing that Broadcom sees VMware as a valuable asset rather than just a numbers-driven deal. He highlighted VMware's early bet on multicloud starting in 2018 and the introduction of VMware Aria, a true multicloud management solution that federates data sources in real-time using GraphQL. Raghuram stressed that Aria differentiates itself by pulling data directly across various clouds, rather than acting as an interface for separate products.

title: HP, Dell report cooling PC sales as business demand wavers
summary: HP and Dell have reported a decline in enterprise demand for PCs, signaling the end of the pandemic-era boom in the PC market. This downturn is attributed to economic uncertainty, with businesses delaying and reducing orders. Despite the current cooling, analysts believe the PC market's long-term floor is higher than pre-COVID levels due to hybrid work and hardware replacement cycles.

title: Backblaze thinks SSDs are more reliable than hard drives
summary: Cloud backup provider Backblaze has released new data suggesting that SSDs are more reliable than HDDs, particularly when used as boot drives in their data centers. This finding supports anecdotal evidence, though Backblaze cautions that SSD reliability could diminish with age and media wear-out limits. The company continues to monitor drive performance and makes its raw data publicly available for further analysis.

title: HPE expands Alletra with lower prices and more options
summary: HPE has expanded its Alletra block storage line by introducing the hybrid Alletra 5000 array, targeting customers who need lower costs and increased capacity rather than the highest performance. This new model, built on Nimble technology and available as a standalone unit or via HPE GreenLake, utilizes both flash and spinning disks and comes with data reduction benefits. The expansion aims to offer more consumption options and make storage-as-a-service more accessible to a broader range of organizations.

title: Mike Cullen Leads N-able RMM Software Business for MSP Partners
summary: N-able has appointed Mike Cullen as the general manager of its RMM (remote monitoring and management) business, focusing on MSP partners. Cullen, an industry veteran, previously helped build N-able from its early days around 2000 and has navigated multiple ownership changes. In his new role, he will oversee the strategic direction of N-able’s RMM platforms and complementary portfolio to accelerate growth in the competitive RMM market.

title: Team Liquid partners with Thorne in growing focus on player health and training
summary: Team Liquid has partnered with Thorne HealthTech to enhance player health and training, focusing on optimising performance through at-home health tests, nutritional supplements, and quizzes. This multi-year deal aims to support both professional players and casual fans with science-backed products for sleep, mental focus, and energy levels. The initiative is part of Team Liquid's broader focus on transforming the training experience in esports, complementing existing programs like The Pro Lab.

title: The Robots are Coming: Inside Uber Eats’ Autonomous Future
summary: Uber Eats is piloting autonomous robotic delivery units in West Hollywood, with Australian Dean Apostolopoulos leading the initiative. These robots, like "Sydney," are designed to complement human delivery drivers, not replace them, addressing growing demand and offering a new customer experience. The project emphasizes sustainability with zero-emission batteries and represents Uber's strategic move into global logistics and food delivery, with future partnerships planned to expand autonomous services.

title: Enterprise Risk Management Global Market Report 2022
summary: The global enterprise risk management market is projected to grow from $4.46 billion in 2021 to $6.20 billion in 2026, driven by an increasing surge in data security breaches within enterprises. Key market players include IBM, Capgemini, and Oracle, with strategic partnerships emerging as a significant trend to leverage resources and expand market presence. North America led the market in 2021, and Asia Pacific is anticipated to be the fastest-growing region.

title: 7 of the best pre-built gaming PCs: Why we love Corsair, Lenovo, Dell, and more
summary: This article reviews seven of the best pre-built gaming PCs from brands like Corsair, Lenovo, and Dell, highlighting their strengths and ideal use cases. It provides a comprehensive guide on what to consider when purchasing a gaming PC, including components like CPU, storage, memory, and graphics. The article also offers specific recommendations for various budgets and performance needs.

title: How John Deere built its own cellular network for its factory
summary: John Deere has built its own private cellular network for its Moline, Illinois factory after purchasing 50 megahertz of wireless spectrum. This initiative is part of a larger plan to modernize its manufacturing facilities by transitioning from wired Ethernet and Wi-Fi to a more reliable and reconfigurable wireless infrastructure, eventually aiming for a custom 5G network on its CBRS spectrum. The company anticipates significant growth in factory sensors and believes this private network will enable new use cases, save costs, and improve operational flexibility and data collection.

title: NetApp: Storage Portfolio Review
summary: This article provides a comprehensive review of NetApp's storage offerings, covering its portfolio, partnerships, use cases, and market position. It highlights NetApp's hybrid cloud, unified storage, NVMe solutions, Keystone Storage-as-a-service, StorageGRID, and ONTAP data management software. The review also includes user feedback, industry recognition, and NetApp's competitors in the data storage market.

title: Data Center: From the real to the virtual
summary: The article discusses the shift from physical to virtual data centers, driven by advancements in cloud computing and the demand for flexible IT solutions. It explains how data center virtualization works by converting physical centers into digital ones via cloud software, enabling remote access. The piece also differentiates virtualization from cloud computing, highlights the growing market for data center virtualization, and details its benefits such as scalability, data mobility, and cost savings.

title: Samsung, Qualcomm unveil industry’s fastest LPDDR5X DRAM chip
summary: Samsung Electronics Co. has successfully demonstrated the performance of its LPDDR5X DRAM on Qualcomm’s Snapdragon mobile platform, achieving an industry-leading speed of 8.5 gigabits per second (Gbps). This advancement surpasses Samsung's previous record and solidifies its leadership in the memory market. The new high-speed, low-power memory is expected to enhance mobile, gaming, camera, and AI applications and find broader use in PCs, HPC, servers, and autonomous driving.

title: Plaintiffs File Amended Complaint in Hard Drive Component Antitrust Suit
summary: Several dozen plaintiffs have filed a third amended class action complaint against ten hard disk drive (HDD) suspension assembly manufacturers from China, Japan, and Thailand. The suit alleges a global conspiracy to fix prices and allocate market shares for HDD suspension assemblies, which are vital components in electronic storage devices. The plaintiffs seek to recover damages for increased prices paid by consumers during the class period, leveraging previous guilty pleas secured by the Department of Justice against some defendants.

title: What to expect during AMD’s 4th Gen EPYC Genoa Release: Join theCUBE Nov. 10
summary: AMD is set to release its 4th Gen EPYC Genoa CPU on November 10, entering a competitive x86 processor market. The new chips are expected to feature 96 cores and 192 threads based on a 5-nanometer manufacturing process, offering higher density and power efficiency. This launch positions AMD ahead of competitor Intel, which has delayed its Xeon Scalable CPUs.

title: Samsung, LG gear up for launch of 8K gaming monitors
summary: Samsung and LG are preparing to launch 8K gaming monitors, with Samsung unveiling its Odyssey Neo G9 at CES 2023. This move comes as gamers seek larger, clearer screens and high-performance GPUs enable 8K quality. However, high prices and a lack of compatible games remain challenges for mainstream adoption, despite an expected significant growth in the 8K device market.

title: HPE GreenLake for VMware Cloud Integrated Solution Announced
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise and VMware have announced HPE GreenLake for VMware, an integrated solution designed to offer a pay-as-you-go hybrid cloud consumption model. This offering, unveiled at VMware Explore 2022 Europe, aims to accelerate deployment and simplify operational complexity for customers looking for a turnkey hybrid cloud solution. It allows organizations to deploy VMware Cloud environments on-premises, in colocation facilities, or at the edge, all managed by the HPE GreenLake platform, and is expected to be available in the first half of 2023.

title: Micron Ships Data Center-Class DDR5, Ready For New AMD EPYC CPUs
summary: Micron Technology announced it is shipping its new data center-class DDR5 SDRAM memory in volume, coinciding with the release of AMD's new Zen 4 EPYC processors. This transition from DDR4 to DDR5 is described as a "once-in-a-decade change," crucial for handling the increasing data volumes and performance demands of modern data centers. The new DDR5 memory offers increased bandwidth, more channels, and improved power efficiency compared to its predecessor.

title: ‘Confident in our strategy’: Rackspace CEO optimistic for success of company’s reorganization
summary: Rackspace Technology Inc.'s new CEO, Amar Maletira, is optimistic about the company's reorganization efforts, despite heavy lifting ahead and a widening net loss in the third quarter. The company aims to regain investor credibility and achieve profitable growth by repositioning its operations into separate business units for private and public clouds. This transition year also includes a move from its longtime "Castle" headquarters to a smaller office space reflecting a shift to hybrid work.

title: HPE's low-end supercomputers take aim at the AI market
summary: HPE and Dell are introducing new supercomputing and high-performance computing (HPC) systems designed to meet the growing demands of AI-driven workloads. HPE is focusing on smaller, lower-cost supercomputers with advanced cooling, while Dell is enhancing its HPC offerings with powerful GPUs, aiming to make supercomputing capabilities more accessible to a broader range of enterprise users. This convergence of supercomputing and HPC is blurring the lines between the two, as both companies compete to provide infrastructure that can efficiently handle complex AI, modeling, and simulation tasks.

title: Dell Drops Classical-Quantum Compute Package, Taps IonQ for Hybrid Cloud Approach
summary: Dell Technologies has launched a new hybrid classical-quantum computing package that includes a quantum emulation platform and access to IonQ’s quantum computers, enabling both on-premise and cloud-based quantum acceleration. This approach allows customers to leverage quantum technology for learning, experimentation, and eventual productization, supporting a range of use cases such as chemistry and materials simulation, machine learning, and natural language processing. Dell's strategy emphasizes the complementary roles of classical and quantum infrastructure, differing from other tech giants that develop proprietary quantum hardware.

title: Prisma SD-WAN Chatbot for Slack Enables Intelligent Operations
summary: Palo Alto Networks has introduced the Prisma SD-WAN Chatbot for Slack CloudBlade, aiming to enhance collaboration and operational efficiency for organizations using SD-WAN solutions. This chatbot allows IT and non-IT staff to query network and application performance information using natural language in Slack, and receive instant notifications for alerts. It simplifies troubleshooting, reduces context switching, and provides secure, anytime, anywhere visibility into the SD-WAN environment.

title: Dell Technologies will pay $1 billion to settle lawsuit over 2018 stock deal
summary: Dell Technologies has agreed to pay $1 billion to settle an investor lawsuit related to its 2018 stock deal that returned the company to public trading. The lawsuit alleged that the transaction for Class V shares was made for billions less than fair value. Industry analysts believe the settlement, though substantial, will not cause long-term financial harm to the tech giant, which is in a strong financial position.

title: Dell reaches $1 billion settlement over disputed 2018 stock swap
summary: Dell Technologies Inc has agreed to a $1 billion all-cash settlement in a lawsuit asserting the company short-changed shareholders in a 2018 stock swap involving VMware. The settlement, which resolves claims against Dell and controlling shareholders including Michael Dell and Silver Lake, is subject to approval by a Delaware Chancery Court judge and will impact Dell's third-quarter results. Plaintiff attorneys have hailed the settlement as the largest cash class-action settlement in a Delaware state court, emphasizing the importance of respecting minority shareholder rights.

title: Dell Technologies to Pay $1 Billion Settlement to Investors Over 2018 Stock Swap
summary: Dell Technologies has agreed to a $1 billion settlement to resolve an investor lawsuit stemming from its 2018 stock swap that returned the company to public trading. The lawsuit, brought by shareholders who claimed they were shortchanged by "billions of dollars below fair value," was scheduled for trial in a Delaware court. This settlement is noted as one of the largest cash settlements in Delaware Chancery Court history for shareholders.

title: IonQ Works with Dell Technologies to Deliver Hybrid Classical-Quantum Platform
summary: IonQ has partnered with Dell Technologies to introduce a hybrid classical-quantum computing platform. This collaboration aims to accelerate innovation by allowing companies to deploy quantum solutions leveraging IonQ's Aria quantum computer and Dell's PowerEdge servers with Qiskit Dell Runtime infrastructure. The platform enables on-premises simulation for less intensive tasks and uses IonQ QPUs for resource-intensive quantum workloads, reducing barriers to quantum research and experimentation across various industries.

title: Ethernet Switch and Router Market Size to Grow by USD 6074.87 million From 2022 to 2027, Assessment on Parent Market, Five Forces Analysis, Market Dynamics & Segmentation - Technavio
summary: The global Ethernet Switch and Router Market is projected to grow by USD 6074.87 million from 2022 to 2027, with a CAGR of 2.67% and a Y-o-Y growth rate of 1.61%. This growth is primarily driven by the increasing demand for colocation data centers among SMEs. North America is expected to contribute 42% to this growth, largely due to the adoption of data centers by various service providers and governments. The market will also see trends like the rising use of open Ethernet switches, despite challenges posed by interoperability issues.

title: Opinion: The holidays won’t end the PC boom-turned-bust, and neither will the new year
summary: The article discusses how the personal computer boom, fueled by the pandemic, is experiencing a downturn that is likely to continue through the holiday season and into 2023. This bust is evidenced by recent earnings reports from major PC makers like HP and Dell, as well as retailer Best Buy, which predict significant declines in PC shipments. While this trend is unfavorable for manufacturers, it could lead to potential bargains for consumers in need of a new computer.

title: HP Inc to reduce global headcount by up to 6,000 employees as demand slows
summary: HP Inc. announced a reduction of 4,000-6,000 employees, representing 7-11% of its global workforce, as part of its "Future Ready Transformation Plan" to achieve $1.4 billion in annual savings by fiscal 2025. This decision comes amidst slowing demand in the PC and printer markets and a volatile macro-environment, with the company reporting fiscal 2022 net revenue of $63 billion, down 0.8% from the previous year. The move follows a general trend of layoffs in the tech sector, as the global PC market experienced significant declines in 2022 after a pandemic-driven boom.

title: Absolute Software enhances resilient zero-trust capabilities
summary: Absolute Software has enhanced its resilient Zero Trust offerings through API expansion, the Absolute Connector for ServiceNow, and an expanded application resilience catalog. These innovations aim to provide users with deeper visibility, intelligence, and software integrations, alongside a refreshed user experience for Absolute Secure Endpoint and Absolute Secure Access. The updates reinforce the company's commitment to enabling organizations to adopt a resilient Zero Trust approach while maintaining security and productivity.

title: Nutanix buyout may be on the cards with HPE sniffing around
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) is reportedly considering acquiring hyperconverged infrastructure vendor Nutanix, with discussions ongoing for several months. The potential acquisition aligns with HPE's GreenLake everything-as-a-service (XaaS) platform strategy, aiming to enhance its software offerings and attract more customers. Nutanix's recent financial recovery and improved market cap make it an attractive target, especially given HPE's struggles in expanding the GreenLake model to its global partners.

title: For HPE, The Cloud Is A Private And A Public Matter
summary: HPE is focusing on its GreenLake utility platform to expand its presence in the hybrid cloud market, emphasizing private cloud offerings. The company aims to make its entire portfolio available as a service and is seeing steady growth in GreenLake's orders and revenue. HPE is enhancing GreenLake with new services like Private Cloud Enterprise and expanding partner offerings, catering to businesses that prefer a mix of public and private cloud solutions, especially for edge computing needs.

title: Micron Delivers the World's Most Advanced Client SSD Featuring 232-Layer NAND Technology
summary: Micron announced the shipment of its 2550 NVMe SSD to global PC OEM customers, marking it as the world's first client SSD to use NAND with over 200 layers (232-layer NAND). This new SSD offers significant performance improvements and power savings, extending battery life for laptops and desktops. The Micron 2550 SSD is available in various form factors and capacities, providing flexibility for system designers.

title: Alex Kim: Why I joined Cloudflare
summary: Alex Kim announced his new role as Cloudflare's Country Manager of South Korea, marking the opening of Cloudflare Korea LLC in Seoul. He outlines his vision for expanding Cloudflare's presence in the Korean market, focusing on accelerating cloud transformation, enhancing security, and supporting Korean companies in globalizing their businesses. Kim emphasizes Cloudflare's potential to become a leading cloud security company in Korea, driven by innovative solutions like R2 and Zero Trust.

title: A dip in Alder Lake with an HP Elitebook is spoiled by avoidable mistakes
summary: The HP Elitebook 1040 x360 g9, featuring an Intel Alder Lake CPU, offers solid performance and thoughtful additions but is marred by several design flaws and software issues. Reviewer Simon Sharwood notes its speed in benchmarks and daily tasks but criticizes the poor keyboard, inconvenient port placement, useless tablet mode, and excessive pre-installed "nagware." Despite its processing power, the laptop ultimately feels uninspired and fails to deliver a "fresh perspective" often sought in the "Desktop Tourism" series.

title: Arqit announces Technology Update
summary: Arqit Quantum Inc. announced a significant change in its technology strategy, moving away from satellites for its core QuantumCloud™ offering due to advancements in terrestrial delivery methods. This shift is expected to positively impact the company's financial position by reducing capital expenditures and generating revenue through licensing its quantum satellite IP to customers with ultra-secure requirements. The company's core product, QuantumCloud™, will now be entirely based on classical digital hardware and software, making it more scalable, efficient, and less risky to operate.

title: Arqit Quantum Inc. Announces Financial and Operational Results for the Fiscal Year 2022
summary: Arqit Quantum Inc. announced its financial and operational results for the fiscal year ended September 30, 2022, reporting $20 million in revenue and other operating income. A significant technological innovation means the company no longer needs to build quantum satellites, leading to expected positive impacts on future financial results and a refocusing of its go-to-market strategy through major channel partnerships. The company also detailed key operational highlights, including integrations with Fortinet, Amazon Web Services, and Dell Technologies, and announced awards and progress in government markets.

title: Corsair Katar Elite Wireless Gaming Mouse Review
summary: The Corsair Katar Elite Wireless Gaming Mouse is reviewed as a strong entry-level option, praised for its lightweight design and versatile connectivity. While offering good value for its price, the reviewer notes some minor issues with side button compatibility in games and suggests it may require fine-tuning to perfectly match user preferences. The review concludes it's a solid choice for those willing to invest time in customization.

title: What Is HP Smart Friend a Complete Overview | HP® Tech Takes
summary: HP SmartFriend is a subscription-based tech support service offering 24/7 access to experts for troubleshooting, maintenance, and setup of tech devices from any brand. It provides various plans, including individual, family, and a one-time fee option, covering services like software installation, network troubleshooting, virus removal, and PC migration. The service aims to save users time and money compared to traditional repair shops by offering affordable, reliable, and accessible remote assistance.

title: ReMarkets to Promote Jake Player as CEO
summary: ReMarkets, a provider of lifecycle management solutions for technology assets, announced that its founder and CEO, Jeff Zeigler, will retire at the end of 2022 after nearly three decades in the industry. Jake Player, who has served as Senior Vice President since 2019, will be promoted to Chief Executive Officer. Brightstar Capital Partners, the majority owner of ReMarkets, expressed confidence in Player's experience to guide the company's future growth.

title: What's next: Predictions, challenges and IT trends for 2023
summary: Despite a challenging economic climate, businesses anticipate increased IT spending in 2023, particularly in cybersecurity, AI, and automation, driven by ongoing skills shortages and growing IT complexity. Organizations that have embraced digital transformation are keen to continue investing, seeing long-term gains even amid economic downturns. This shift also redefines the business-IT relationship, with IT operations becoming central to achieving business success and strategic initiatives.

title: Nvidia debuts new DGX H100 systems powered by Intel’s 4th Gen Intel Xeon Scalable chips
summary: Nvidia has unveiled new DGX H100 systems that integrate its H100 Tensor Core GPUs with Intel's recently launched 4th Gen Intel Xeon Scalable processors. These systems, along with upcoming server offerings from Nvidia's partners, promise significantly enhanced efficiency and performance for AI workloads, boasting up to 25 times more efficiency and 3.5 times better AI training/inference efficiency compared to previous generation machines. The new hardware leverages PCIe Gen 5 for faster data transfer and includes a free license for Nvidia Enterprise AI software.

title: NetApp appoints new CMO
summary: NetApp has appointed Gabrielle Boko as its new Chief Marketing Officer (CMO), replacing James Whitemore. Boko, who previously served as NetApp's SVP of Portfolio Marketing, brings extensive experience in software and cloud marketing from her previous roles at HPE, SAP, and Sage. Her appointment signals NetApp's continued focus on evolving as a cloud-led, data-centric software company.

title: IDC, Gartner: 2022 PC Demand Dropped To Its ‘Lowest Level In Years’
summary: PC demand in 2022 dropped significantly to its lowest level in years, according to reports from IDC and Gartner. This decline was attributed to global recession, inflation, higher interest rates, and consumers already owning newer PCs purchased during the pandemic. The business market too saw a decline as enterprise buyers extended PC life cycles and delayed purchases, with growth not expected until 2024.

title: What organizations miss in their digitization strategies
summary: Many organizations are failing to fully leverage the potential of data in their digitization strategies, leading to a "new type of digital divide." A Hewlett Packard Enterprise survey found the average organization's data maturity score was only 2.6 out of five, indicating a lack of basic capabilities to create value from data. To address this, organizations must adopt a "data first" mentality, prioritize data control, and implement strategic investments, dedicated leadership, and robust architectures to unlock data's transformative power for economic and social progress.

title: Dell announces new PowerEdge servers based on Intel's 4th Gen Xeon Scalable processors
summary: Dell Technologies Inc. has refreshed its PowerEdge server lineup with 13 new models featuring Intel Corp.’s 4th Generation Xeon Scalable processors. These new servers, including rack, tower, and multi-node options, boast improved performance, reliability, and energy efficiency for various computing environments, from data centers to the network edge. Dell also highlighted enhanced server management software and introduced these servers as part of an expanded Dell APEX "as-a-service" offering.

title: A2Z Smart Technologies agrees Cust2mate smart cart partnership with Lenovo Group
summary: A2Z Smart Technologies Corp. subsidiary, Cust2mate, has announced a partnership with Lenovo Group. Under this joint venture, Cust2mate will integrate Lenovo solutions into its smart cart technology, and Lenovo will facilitate sales and promotion globally. This collaboration aims to expand Cust2mate's reach into new markets, leveraging Lenovo's worldwide support and enabling a seamless shopping experience for customers.

title: Choice Hotels Opens Cambria Hotel Austin Uptown Near the Domain
summary: Choice Hotels International has opened the Cambria Hotel Austin Uptown Near the Domain, marking its second property in Austin, Texas, with a third on the way. The 135-room hotel is strategically located in Austin's high-tech corridor, providing convenient access to major tech companies and local attractions. It offers modern amenities and design-forward accommodations aimed at both business and leisure travelers.

title: Dell acquires Cloud services startup Cloudify for '$100 million'
summary: Dell Technologies has acquired the Israeli startup Cloudify for an estimated $100 million. Cloudify specializes in cloud orchestration and infrastructure automation, and this acquisition aims to enhance Dell's cloud services, particularly in DevOps offerings. Cloudify's open-source, multi-cloud orchestration platform is known for packaging infrastructure, networking, and automation tools into certified blueprints.
