summary: Cisco plans to lay off 7% of its global workforce, approximately 5,600 employees, as part of a restructuring to invest in key growth areas like artificial intelligence, cloud, and cybersecurity. CFO Scott Herren stated this is a reallocation of resources rather than a cost-cutting measure. The company anticipates up to $1 billion in restructuring costs and reported a 10% decline in revenues for its fiscal 2024 fourth quarter.

title: Why Legendary Investor Bill Miller Still Loves Bitcoin — And 3 Ways To Beat The Market
summary: Legendary investor Bill Miller III shares his continued bullishness on Bitcoin, detailing how he got into the cryptocurrency and why he views it as an insurance policy against financial catastrophe. The article also provides insights into his market-beating strategies over 15 years, emphasizing key lessons learned from past successes with Amazon and Dell and disappointments with Kodak. Miller discusses the importance of an analytical or behavioral edge in investing and recommends essential readings for aspiring investors.

title: AMD's $4.9 Billion Acquisition of ZT Systems: A Strategic Move to Challenge Nvidia's AI Dominance
summary: AMD has announced its US$4.9 billion acquisition of ZT Systems, a leading provider of AI infrastructure. This strategic move aims to strengthen AMD's position in the AI infrastructure market and enable it to better compete with industry leader Nvidia by offering comprehensive, optimized AI solutions to cloud and enterprise customers. The acquisition is intended to combine AMD's silicon and software capabilities with ZT Systems' expertise in designing and deploying data center AI compute and storage infrastructure.

title: AI-First Approach to Cancer Diagnostics Earns Ibex Medical Analytics Notable Milestones
summary: Ibex Medical Analytics announced significant advancements highlighted by a 178% surge in new customers, successful validation through a Quest Diagnostics study, and regulatory approvals including IVDR certification for its AI solutions. The company also expanded its Board of Directors with the addition of Ann Costello. These milestones underscore Ibex's leadership in AI-powered cancer diagnostics and its commitment to improving diagnostic accuracy and efficiency for pathologists worldwide.

title: HP Computers Made with Ocean-Bound Plastics | Sustainable Tech
summary: HP is actively addressing the global plastic pollution crisis by incorporating ocean-bound plastics into its products through its circular supply chain and HP Planet Partners program. The company defines ocean-bound plastics as uncollected plastic waste within 30 miles of waterways, aiming to stop it from reaching the ocean. HP's initiative also focuses on social impact, creating employment opportunities and community support in areas like Haiti, while showcasing various products made with these recycled materials.

title: Optimize your VMware environment with new capabilities
summary: NetApp and VMware are strengthening their partnership to help customers optimize their VMware environments, especially in light of the shift to new VMware by Broadcom subscriptions. They are introducing joint solutions, including expanded use of NetApp ONTAP for VMware Cloud Foundation, next-generation vVols engineering, and support for Spot Eco by NetApp with Azure VMware Solution. These initiatives aim to help CIOs rightsize their VMware investments, reduce costs by 25-50%, and improve data management efficiency.

title: What is NetApp?
summary: NetApp is a multinational corporation specializing in data storage hardware, software, and cloud services, known for its unified storage offerings and hybrid cloud solutions. The article details NetApp's history, financial performance, product portfolio including prominent storage systems, and key acquisitions. It also touches on a past Department of Justice probe and leadership changes throughout its existence.

title: Classroom quitters to industry leaders: These 14 billionaires dropped out of school to chase success
summary: This article highlights 14 billionaires who dropped out of formal education to pursue their entrepreneurial dreams and achieve significant success. It spotlights individuals like Ralph Lauren, Oprah Winfrey, Bill Gates, and Mark Zuckerberg, detailing their early career decisions and current net worth according to Forbes. The piece illustrates that success is not always linear and can be achieved through ambition and unconventional paths.

title: Sify becomes India’s first NVIDIA DGX Ready DC Partner certified for Liquid Cooling
summary: Sify Technologies Limited has become India's first NVIDIA DGX-Ready Data Center partner certified for liquid cooling, specifically designed to support 130 KW/rack capacity and comply with ASHRAE ambient temperature guidelines. This certification enables Sify to offer NVIDIA DGX-powered colocation solutions and advanced AI software, meeting the growing demand for high-density AI infrastructure in India's rapidly expanding artificial intelligence market. The company will roll out these services from its Mumbai-Rabale campus and upcoming facilities in Noida and Chennai-Siruseri.

title: Tech giant Nvidia is said to be looking for a large office space in Austin area
summary: Nvidia is reportedly seeking a large office space in the Austin area, specifically on the north side of the metro, which would place it near Dell Technologies Inc.'s operations in Round Rock. This move comes as the AI chipmaker, which provides a comprehensive suite of AI development tools, has a strong existing partnership with Dell. The search for new office space highlights Nvidia's potential expansion in the Austin region.

title: VF Corp's Comeback Story: Supreme Sale and Cost Cuts Boost Stock
summary: VF Corp is implementing a "Reinvent Turnaround Strategy" focusing on improving North American results, revitalizing the Vans brand, cutting $300 million in costs, and strengthening its balance sheet through asset sales like the $1.5 billion divestiture of Supreme. Despite a Q1 2025 loss, the company shows progress in inventory reduction and debt and maintains its full-year outlook. The stock has seen a surge, but analysts advise caution, suggesting pullbacks for bullish investors.

title: Oracle Offers First Zettascale Cloud Computing Cluster
summary: Oracle has announced the first zettascale cloud computing clusters, accelerated by the NVIDIA Blackwell platform, which will offer up to 131,072 NVIDIA GPUs to create the largest AI supercomputer in the cloud. These OCI Superclusters will deliver unprecedented performance for demanding AI workloads, with current and upcoming configurations supporting NVIDIA H100, H200, and Blackwell GPUs. Customers like WideLabs and Zoom are already leveraging OCI's high-performing AI infrastructure and sovereignty controls for their large language models and generative AI assistants.

title: Jabil Inc to invest Rs 2,000 crore in Trichy
summary: Jabil Inc, a key supplier to Apple and others, is set to invest Rs 2,000 crore in Trichy, Tamil Nadu. This significant investment is expected to create 5,000 jobs and foster a new electronics manufacturing cluster in the region, as announced by Tamil Nadu Chief Minister M.K. Stalin.

title: Salesforce buys Israeli startup Zoomin to feed more data to its AI agents
summary: Salesforce Inc. has acquired Israeli startup Zoomin Software Ltd. for approximately $450 million to enhance its AI agents with unstructured data processing capabilities. Zoomin's technology will be integrated into Salesforce's Agentforce platform, allowing AI agents to deliver more accurate and autonomous responses by leveraging organizational data. This acquisition aims to differentiate Salesforce's AI offerings from competitors like Microsoft by improving the accuracy and utility of its AI agents.

title: Broadcom: Semiconductors, M&A's, and Bold Leadership
summary: This article details Broadcom's journey from its origins in HP's division and Broadcom Corporation to becoming a global semiconductor giant through strategic mergers and acquisitions. It highlights CEO Hock Tan's leadership in expanding the company's product portfolio across semiconductors, software, and infrastructure solutions. The company's strategy involves targeting niche markets and utilizing a fabless design model to achieve market dominance.

title: Michael Dell Sells $1.2B Worth Of Dell Stock, Reducing Stake Amid Company's Recent S&P 500 Inclusion And AI Expansion
summary: Michael Dell, CEO of Dell Technologies Inc., sold 10 million shares of Class C Common Stock for over $1.2 billion, reducing his stake in the company. This comes shortly after Dell's inclusion in the S&P 500 Index and amid CEO Dell's vocal support for generative AI's rapid advancement. Despite the sale, the company's stock has surged 58.50% year-to-date.

title: Denso’s development of “human-like” robots using Azure OpenAI Service as the brain to help robots and humans work together through dialogue
summary: Denso is developing "human-like" robots that can understand human language and autonomously act, powered by Microsoft's Azure OpenAI Service. This initiative aims to overcome the inflexibility of traditional robots and enable seamless human-robot collaboration in diverse environments. The collaboration between Denso's hardware and software expertise and Microsoft's generative AI technology is set to revolutionize robot applications.

title: Dell to Ship Servers With New Nvidia Blackwell AI Chips Next Month
summary: Dell Technologies Inc. is set to begin shipping servers equipped with Nvidia Corp.'s Blackwell AI accelerators to select customers next month, with general availability expected in early 2025. This indicates that the production of Nvidia's Blackwell chips is back on schedule, addressing previous concerns. Arthur Lewis, Dell infrastructure unit president, confirmed this development in an interview.

title: VMware pays up $102.5M in securities fraud settlement
summary: VMware has agreed to pay $102.5 million to settle a shareholder class-action lawsuit alleging securities fraud by creating an artificial sales backlog to hide slowing sales between 2018 and 2020. This settlement follows a similar, but much smaller, case with the US Securities and Exchange Commission in 2022. The article also touches on Broadcom's acquisition of VMware and the subsequent controversial changes to its licensing model, leading to customer backlash and a lawsuit from AT&T.

title: Rockwell Automation names Brian Hovey Chief Marketing Officer
summary: Rockwell Automation (NYSE: ROK) has appointed Brian Hovey as its new Vice President, Global Marketing and Chief Marketing Officer. Hovey will lead global marketing efforts, focusing on driving demand and building the brand, overseeing various marketing functions. He brings over 25 years of leadership experience from companies like Honeywell and Dell Technologies.

title: Goodwill of Southwestern Pennsylvania - Pittsburgh Business Times
summary: This article provides a detailed overview of Goodwill of Southwestern Pennsylvania, covering its mission, history, services, and community impact. It highlights the organization's efforts in workforce development, disability services, and environmental sustainability, as well as its upcoming fundraising events. Key leadership, board members, and contact information are also included.

title: Best Buy confirms all stores will be closed for 24 hours in the coming weeks forcing shoppers to switch up their plans
summary: Best Buy announced that all its stores will be closed on Thanksgiving Day 2024, joining a growing trend among major retailers to prioritize employee well-being. Shoppers can still access Black Friday deals online, with options for free delivery and curbside pickup. The article also mentions Best Buy's recent change to a 15-day return policy, which has caused some customer dissatisfaction, and promotes their tiered membership program for extended return windows.

title: Modine opens Bradford precision cooling factory
summary: Modine has officially opened its new 29,000m² factory in Bradford, UK, dedicated to manufacturing precision cooling products for data centers under the Airedale by Modine brand. This new facility, which specializes in computer room air handlers and fan walls, boosts the company's UK data center cooling production capability by 150% and commenced operations two months ahead of schedule. The investment includes a 2MW test center and is part of Modine's global expansion strategy to meet the strong demand for energy-efficient data center cooling.

title: 2 'Strong Buy' Silver Miners for Penny Stock Speculators
summary: Citi analysts have increased their gold and silver price forecasts, predicting gold could reach $2,800-$3,000 per ounce and silver $40 per ounce, driven by a worsening U.S. labor market and anticipated Fed interest rate cuts. This outlook benefits silver mining stocks, with two "Strong Buy" rated penny stocks, Avino Silver and Gold Mines Ltd. (ASM) and Vizsla Silver Corp. (VZLA), highlighted for significant upside potential. Avino Silver recently reported record Q2 revenue and strong earnings, while Vizsla Silver is a pre-revenue company targeting silver production by late 2027 with substantial cash reserves.

title: Regeneron Set to Regenerate
summary: Regeneron is expanding its operations both in its home state of New York and internationally in Ireland. The biotech company plans significant investments to boost manufacturing capacity and headquarters space in Rensselaer and Tarrytown, New York, creating over 700 new jobs. Concurrently, Regeneron is in final negotiations to acquire a former Dell facility in Limerick, Ireland, to establish a second industrial operations site, aligning with Ireland's growing pharmaceutical sector.

title: Broadcom Unveils VeloRAIN, an Industry-First for Robust AI Networking Beyond the Data Center
summary: Broadcom (NASDAQ: AVGO) announced significant advancements to its VeloCloud portfolio, including the new VeloRAIN architecture for robust AI networking, and the VeloCloud Edge 4100 and 5100 appliances. The company also launched Titan, a new partner program for VeloCloud Managed Service Providers, and published the "State of the Edge" report. These initiatives aim to support the growing demands of AI and non-AI workloads distributed across enterprise networks.

title: Sprinklr Appoints Rory Read as Chief Executive Officer
summary: Sprinklr (NYSE: CXM) has appointed Rory Read as its new President & Chief Executive Officer and a member of its Board of Directors, effective November 5, 2024. Read succeeds Ragy Thomas, who will transition to an Advisor to the CEO role while retaining his position as Founder and Chairman of the Board. Read brings extensive leadership experience from major technology companies like Vonage, Dell, AMD, IBM, and Lenovo, and is expected to lead Sprinklr through its next phase of growth with a focus on its AI-powered platform.

title: Corsair Gaming, Inc.'s (NASDAQ:CRSR) market cap touched US$717m last week, benefiting both private equity firms who own 54% as well as institutions
summary: Corsair Gaming, Inc.'s market capitalization reached US$717 million last week, significantly benefiting private equity firms, which hold 54% ownership, and institutional investors, who own 30%. EagleTree Capital, LP is identified as the largest shareholder with 54% of outstanding shares. The article details the ownership structure, including insights into institutional, insider, and general public ownership, highlighting the influence of private equity.

title: Vertiv Stock Could Keep Soaring After 175% Pop
summary: Vertiv stock has surged 175% following strong third-quarter results and raised guidance, driven by increasing demand for data centers due to AI growth. The company is a market leader in data center liquid cooling and electrical equipment, with analysts bullish on its future prospects. Continued growth in AI development and data center spending is expected to further boost Vertiv's performance.

title: Cisco delivers solid earnings beat, but revenue declines again
summary: Cisco reported solid fiscal first-quarter financial results, beating analyst estimates for earnings and revenue, but saw a fourth successive quarter of declining revenue. Despite raising its 2025 full-year revenue outlook and highlighting growth in security and observability, the networking giant's stock traded lower after-hours due to investor disappointment over the overall revenue decline, especially in its core networking business. The company also emphasized its growing involvement in AI infrastructure orders and acquisitions of AI-focused security startups, though analysts remain skeptical about long-term growth strategies.

title: Broadcom’s VMware strategy is winning despite market friction
summary: Broadcom's acquisition of VMware is succeeding despite media and competitor narratives focusing on increased license fees. The strategy involves narrowing VMware's focus, increasing contract values through the VMware Cloud Foundation (VCF) bundle, and integrating VMware into Broadcom’s business model. While some customers are migrating low-value workloads, most mission-critical work remains, leading to significant revenue increases and a long-term win for customers, competitors, and Broadcom.

title: Super Micro faces deadline to keep Nasdaq listing after 85% plunge in stock
summary: Super Micro Computer is facing a crucial Monday deadline to submit a viable compliance plan to Nasdaq, or risk delisting, following an 85% plunge in its stock value this year. The company is late in filing its 2024 year-end report and has yet to find a replacement for its accounting firm. This situation, coupled with past compliance issues, could damage Super Micro's reputation and potentially benefit competitors like Dell or HPE.

title: This Insurance Giant Has Cut Its Office Budget By $244 Million in 4 Years — Here's Where Employees Will Meet Instead
summary: Allstate has significantly cut its annual spending on corporate offices by $244 million over four years, reducing its real estate footprint from 12 million square feet to 4 million. To accommodate its workforce, Allstate is implementing a new strategy where 25% of its 54,000 corporate employees will utilize coworking spaces for meetings and team-building, a less strict approach compared to companies like Amazon and Dell. This "testing and learning" phase allows the insurance giant to manage in-person gatherings in cities where a full office lease isn't necessary.

title: Applied Digital has emerged as a company to watch as Nvidia discloses stake in the company
summary: Applied Digital (APLD) has seen its shares rally 90% in the last six months, driven by a strategic shift to high-performance computing and a new investment from Nvidia. Nvidia disclosed a 3% stake in APLD, valued at $63.66 million, which bolsters Applied Digital's financial position and highlights its potential in the AI-driven economy. While Applied Digital faces significant investment and remains in a high-growth phase with ongoing net losses, its strong revenue growth and Nvidia's backing suggest further upside potential, though investors should be aware of the inherent volatility of small-cap stocks in rapidly evolving sectors.

title: Penguin Solutions OriginAI Infrastructure Now Available with Additional GPUs and Enhanced Cluster Management Capabilities
summary: Penguin Solutions has expanded its OriginAI infrastructure solution to include the latest NVIDIA and AMD GPUs, as well as Dell Technologies AI-optimized hardware. This enhanced offering provides organizations with more options for tailoring AI infrastructure to specific workloads, supported by Penguin's ClusterWare software and end-to-end services. The updates aim to help companies rapidly deploy AI at scale and achieve faster ROI and competitive benefits.

title: AI factories are requiring better cooling, more efficient systems
summary: The article discusses the collaboration between Denvr Dataworks Inc., Broadcom Inc., and Dell Technologies Inc. to address the challenges of building efficient AI factories that require massive compute power. They are focusing on better cooling systems, such as liquid immersion and liquid-to-chip cooling, and open networking solutions like Ethernet to handle the power-hungry AI workloads. The goal is to create modular, interoperable, and scalable AI infrastructure with efficient power usage and a compact footprint, supported by a full software AI stack.

title: HPE GreenLake Platform Gets Expanded Storage And Custom KVM Hypervisor
summary: HPE has expanded its GreenLake platform with new storage capabilities, including the exabyte-scale Alletra Storage MP X10000 which adds object storage, and a custom KVM hypervisor called VM Essentials. These additions aim to meet the growing demands of organizations adopting hybrid cloud and AI technologies, with GreenLake's customer base and revenue run rate steadily increasing. HPE also introduced a disconnected cloud environment for enhanced data security and regulatory compliance.

title: Amid data boom, Equinix engages in layoffs and restructuring
summary: Equinix confirmed that it recently laid off approximately 3% of its global workforce, or around 400 positions, despite a projected 35% increase in data center spending this year driven by AI. This restructuring includes the decision to shutter its Metal service in 2026 and comes as new CEO Adaire Fox-Martin appears to be reorganizing operations, leading to several executive departures. The company, which is investing up to $15 billion in AI infrastructure, saw its revenues grow 7% to $2.2 billion in its most recent quarter.

title: AI networking solutions drive scalable, efficient systems
summary: Broadcom is leading the charge in AI networking solutions, focusing on energy-efficient chips and scalable networks to meet the escalating demands of AI and high-performance computing. At SC24, Broadcom leaders highlighted innovations like the Tomahawk 5 chip and Thor 2 NIC, which significantly reduce power consumption while maintaining performance. The company emphasizes collaboration with partners like Dell Technologies Inc. and Denver Dataworks Corp. to create open, scalable systems with Ethernet as the foundational "glue" for managing complex AI clusters.

title: Corning offers to waive exclusive deals in EU antitrust probe, may stave off fine
summary: Corning has offered to drop all exclusive dealing clauses in its agreements with mobile phone makers and raw glass processing companies to resolve an EU antitrust investigation. This move could help the company avoid a significant fine. The European Commission will now open a six-week feedback period for third parties before deciding whether to accept Corning's concessions, which would last for nine years.

title: Nvidia leads $700M investment in ex-Yandex CEO Arkady Volozh’s Nebius Group to fuel AI infrastructure growth
summary: Nebius Group, founded by former Yandex CEO Arkady Volozh, has secured $700 million in a private placement led by Nvidia, Accel, and Orbis Investments to expand its AI infrastructure. This funding will enable Nebius to scale GPU clusters and cloud platforms to meet increasing AI demand. The company plans to invest $1 billion by mid-2025 and has significantly raised its annualized run-rate revenue forecast.

title: VTEX Connect Live NYC: Igniting the Future of Global Commerce
summary: VTEX (NYSE: VTEX) is hosting VTEX Connect NYC on January 13, 2025, an event expected to gather 1,400 attendees for insights into global commerce and brand strategy. The conference will feature speakers like Simone Biles, Rebeca Andrade, and VTEX Co-CEO Mariano Gomide de Faria, who will discuss consumer shopping behavior and the future of unified commerce. Following editions in Mexico City and Barcelona, this New York event aims to provide networking opportunities and data-driven strategies for industry leaders.

title: Veeam announces $2B secondary share offering at $15B valuation
summary: Veeam, a leader in data resilience, has announced a $2 billion secondary share offering led by TPG, valuing the company at $15 billion. This move provides Veeam with enhanced flexibility for strategic partnerships, acquisitions, and capitalization opportunities. The company reported $1.7 billion in annualized recurring revenue with 18% year-over-year growth, highlighting its strong financial performance and its focus on cloud-first offerings as data protection evolves with digital transformation and AI adoption.

title: Why Penguin Solutions Could Be the AI Sleeper Stock of 2025
summary: The article discusses Penguin Solutions (NASDAQ: PENG), formerly SMART Global Holdings, as a potential AI sleeper stock due to its focus on AI and high-performance computing (HPC) and its low forward P/E multiple. It details Penguin's business segments, particularly its Advanced Computing segment and a recent collaboration with Dell, while acknowledging recent revenue declines and the cyclical nature of the semiconductor industry. The author suggests that as the AI revolution progresses, Penguin's "nice-to-have" offerings could become essentials, making it a company to watch.

title: Green Data Center (GDC) Market to grow by USD 202.4 Billion from 2024-2028, driven by rising electricity costs and AI redefining the market landscape - Technavio
summary: The global Green Data Center (GDC) market is projected to grow by USD 202.4 billion from 2024-2028, largely driven by increasing electricity costs and the transformative impact of AI. This growth is expected at a CAGR of 27.17% during the forecast period, with key market trends highlighting energy efficiency, environmental stewardship, and the integration of renewable energy sources and advanced DCIM solutions. Despite challenges related to the cost of building these centers, the demand for GDCs is accelerating due to digital transformation, environmental regulations, and corporate social responsibility initiatives across various industries.

title: CoreWeave and Dell Technologies Extend Relationship to Deliver AI at Scale
summary: Dell Technologies and CoreWeave are expanding their strategic relationship to deliver AI at scale, with CoreWeave being the first to ship Dell PowerEdge XE9712 server racks with NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 to support its Cloud Services Platform. This collaboration integrates Dell's infrastructure and services with NVIDIA's accelerated computing, networking, and AI software to meet the growing demand for high-performance cloud environments for generative AI developments. CoreWeave will leverage Dell's fully integrated and validated rack systems and professional services to accelerate implementation and enhance its specialized AI cloud services platform.

title: Silver Lake Sells $12.9 Million Dell Technologies Stake Amid 83% Stock Surge
summary: Silver Lake, a major shareholder in Dell Technologies, recently sold $12.9 million worth of Dell's Class C Common Stock following an 83% stock surge over the past year. Despite the sale, Silver Lake maintains a significant indirect holding in Dell. The transaction occurred as Dell reported strong third-quarter revenue driven by AI-based server demand, with earnings and cash dividends increasing.

title: How AI agents are the next wave of generative technology
summary: AI agents are emerging as the next significant phase in generative AI, drawing both excitement and caution from industry leaders. While experts like Tim Cotten foresee widespread adoption, emphasizing their ability to automate tasks and generate new ideas, concerns remain regarding their effective implementation, the unknown ROI, and the potential for "agentic hallucinations" if not properly managed. The consensus among experts is that specialized, modular agents overseen by human validation are key to successful deployment.

title: Lenovo, HP And Dell Are Betting Big On A PC Refresh In 2025 (So Is The Channel)
summary: Major PC manufacturers like Lenovo, HP, and Dell, along with their channel partners, are highly optimistic about a significant PC refresh in 2025. This anticipation is driven by several factors, including the Windows 10 end-of-life, the increasing demand for AI-capable PCs, and a large installed base of aging computers purchased during the COVID-19 pandemic. While some industry analysts predict the full surge of AI PC sales might extend into 2026, the consensus among these tech giants and solution providers points to 2025 as a pivotal year for PC market recovery, especially in the commercial sector.

title: Dell, Corning, Coherent: JPMorgan's Top Picks For Cyclical Recovery 'Beyond AI'
summary: JPMorgan analyst Samik Chatterjee identifies Dell Technologies, Corning Inc, and Coherent Corp as top picks for a cyclical recovery in traditional IT infrastructure and industrial tech, moving beyond AI-driven growth. These companies are expected to benefit from recovering demand in servers, telecom, and industrial markets, along with a broader resurgence in networking sectors. The report highlights a robust cyclical resurgence in critical hardware and networking sectors, with companies like Arista Networks, Cisco, and Amphenol also strategically positioned to capitalize on emerging opportunities.

title: ‘Synaptics plans to assemble 10% chips in India’
summary: US semiconductor company Synaptics Inc. plans to assemble and package 10% of its chips in India by 2027 to diversify its supply chain and reduce reliance on China. The company will use an OSAT facility in Hyderabad and partner with embed UR to develop edge AI solutions, aiming for the process to begin in late 2025 or early 2026. This move also addresses concerns about proposed US tariffs and the need for supply chain alternatives.

title: Shriram Finance, Anant Raj, PPL among 9 stocks that saw brokerage initiations with 40% upside
summary: Several brokerage firms have recently initiated coverage on nine buzzing stocks, including Shriram Finance, Anant Raj, and Piramal Pharma, all with 'buy' or 'add' ratings and an upside potential of up to 40%. The brokerages highlight strong growth prospects, expanding market share, and strategic initiatives as key drivers for these companies. Each company's specific investment case, target price, and expected upside are detailed in the report.

title: Equinix Partners with Dell Technologies to Provide Scalable AI Infrastructure for Enterprises
summary: Equinix has partnered with Dell Technologies to introduce a private AI solution designed for enterprises. This solution leverages the Dell AI Factory with NVIDIA within Equinix's IBX data centers, enabling businesses to train AI models with enhanced control, security, and low-latency deployment on-premises. The collaboration aims to provide scalable, cost-efficient, and sustainable AI infrastructure, addressing the growing demand for advanced AI capabilities while ensuring data sovereignty and meeting corporate sustainability goals.

title: Goldman Sachs Picks Arista Networks, Inc. (ANET) as Top Stock for 2025, Boosted by Strong AI Data Center Demand
summary: Goldman Sachs has identified Arista Networks, Inc. (ANET) as a top stock pick for 2025, driven by the robust demand for AI data center equipment. The firm maintains a Buy rating and a $120 price target, citing Arista Networks' early market share leadership in AI switching. This recommendation comes amidst a broader economic landscape where companies are heavily investing in AI development despite warnings about the technology's risks.

title: Qualcomm’s New Chips Will Bring AI to $600 Personal Computers
summary: Qualcomm Inc. introduced its new Snapdragon X Platform chips at CES 2025, designed to power AI-capable personal computers priced as low as $600. These chips feature an 8-core Oryon central processor, a graphics component, and a dedicated AI chip, and will run Microsoft Corp.'s Copilot+ software. Major PC manufacturers like Dell Technologies Inc. and Lenovo Group Ltd. are expected to release laptops using this new technology in early 2025.

title: Dell picks AMD for business PCs
summary: Dell Technologies will start using Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) chips for its business PCs for the first time, a move announced at CES in Las Vegas. This decision is seen as a blow to Intel's dominance in the PC chip market, especially in the more profitable commercial sector. AMD unveiled new Ryzen AI Max series processors and 9000 series desktop processors, claiming significant performance improvements for AI workloads, while competitors Intel and Qualcomm also showcased their AI-focused chips.

title: NVIDIA Announces Blueprint for AI Retail Shopping Assistants
summary: NVIDIA has introduced the NVIDIA AI Blueprint for retail shopping assistants, a generative AI workflow aimed at enhancing online and in-store shopping experiences. This blueprint, built on NVIDIA AI Enterprise and Omniverse platforms, empowers developers to create AI-powered digital assistants capable of tasks like understanding text/image prompts, searching multiple items, and presenting products in virtual environments. The initiative seeks to improve customer satisfaction, boost conversion rates, and reduce returns by providing personalized and intelligent shopping assistance.

title: HPE secures $1 bln AI server deal for Elon Musk's X, Bloomberg News reports
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) has secured a deal exceeding $1 billion to supply AI-optimized servers to Elon Musk's social media platform X. The agreement, reported by Bloomberg News, was finalized late last year. HPE's competitors, Dell Technologies and Super Micro Computer, also reportedly bid for the contract, highlighting the strong demand for AI servers from companies like Musk's X, Tesla, and xAI.

title: Penguin Solution: Strong Growth Outlook With Strategic AI Partnerships (NASDAQ:PENG)
summary: The article expresses a positive outlook on Penguin Solution (PENG) due to its strong position in the AI infrastructure market. The company reported a 24% year-over-year revenue increase, with its Advanced Computing segment growing by 49%, and a significant 108% increase in adjusted EPS. Strategic partnerships with SK Telecom and Dell Technologies are expected to further enhance PENG's competitive edge and market penetration in enterprise AI deployments.

title: Applied Digital Rises 6% in a Week: Buy, Sell, or Hold the Stock?
summary: Applied Digital (APLD) shares have gained 6.3% in the past week, driven by strong top-line growth, especially in its Cloud Services segment. The company is expanding its data center capacity to support AI and HPC applications and has strong partnerships and an improving balance sheet. However, its valuation appears stretched, and the company carries a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold), suggesting a cautious approach for investors.

title: Oracle surges, chip stocks rise as Trump unveils $500 bln AI plan
summary: Oracle's shares jumped nearly 9% after former President Donald Trump announced a $500 billion AI infrastructure investment plan, "Stargate," involving Oracle, OpenAI, and SoftBank to keep the U.S. competitive in the global AI race. Other technology partners and AI-related companies, including Nvidia, Microsoft, Arm, Vistra, Dell Technologies, Super Micro Computer, and Palantir Technologies, also saw their stock prices increase following the news. The plan includes an immediate $100 billion investment, with the remaining over the next four years, and construction of data centers already underway in Texas.

title: Savant Labs Raises $18.5 Million in Series A
summary: Savant Labs, an AI-driven analytics automation platform based in San Mateo, CA, has successfully raised $18.5 million in Series A funding. The round was led by Dell Technologies Capital, with participation from several other investors including Vertex Ventures and Cota Capital. The company plans to use these funds to expand its AI automation capabilities and scale its operations.

title: The importance of diversity in tech teams
summary: This article highlights the critical importance of diversity in tech teams for driving organizational success, innovation, and revenue. It details how diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI) initiatives can improve recruitment, retention, efficiency, and access to talent, while also mitigating legal risks. The article also addresses emerging concerns regarding DEI initiatives and their impact on different generational employees.

title: Is It Too Late To Consider Buying Seagate Technology Holdings plc (NASDAQ:STX)?
summary: Seagate Technology Holdings (NASDAQ:STX) experienced a double-digit share price rise recently but is still trading around 11% below its intrinsic value, appearing fairly valued. The company has a positive outlook with expected profit growth of 18% over the next couple of years. While the market seems to have priced in the positive outlook, its share price volatility might offer future buying opportunities for potential investors.

title: Hyperscale Data Center Market worth $608.54 Billion by 2030- Exclusive Report by MarketsandMarkets™
summary: The global Hyperscale Data Center Market is projected to grow from USD 162.79 billion in 2024 to USD 608.54 billion by 2030, exhibiting a CAGR of 24.6%. This growth is driven by the increasing integration of AI and ML technologies, the demand for advanced hardware to support high-performance computing, and the rise of greenfield deployments for customized and energy-efficient data centers. Cloud providers are expected to dominate the market share due to their extensive investments in hyperscale infrastructure.

title: XR8 Technology Services add new IT partnerships to its growing portfolio of vendors
summary: Northampton-based XR8 Technology Services has announced new partnerships with global technology leaders Cisco and Zebra Technologies, expanding its vendor portfolio. These collaborations aim to provide local Northamptonshire businesses with critical IT solutions, including Cisco's cybersecurity and Zebra's equipment for manufacturing and logistics. This move reinforces XR8's commitment to delivering world-class IT services and expertise to the local business community.

title: NetApp, Inc.'s (NASDAQ:NTAP) Share Price Could Signal Some Risk
summary: NetApp, Inc. (NASDAQ:NTAP) currently has a high P/E ratio of 21.1x, which may indicate bearish signals compared to the broader US market. Despite strong past earnings growth, analysts project future earnings growth for NetApp to be significantly lower than the market average, suggesting that its high P/E ratio might not be sustainable and could lead to future disappointment for investors. The article warns that the company's share price could be at risk of declining unless its growth outlook improves.

title: Data Center Networking Software Market: USD 18.2 billion
summary: The global Data Center Networking Software market is projected to reach USD 18.2 billion by 2031, growing at a CAGR of 7.9% from 2025 to 2031, driven by technological advancements, expanding applications across end-use sectors, favorable government policies, and increased investment in R&D. Key players include Cisco Systems Inc., Juniper Networks Inc., and Arista Networks Inc. The market is segmented by software type, deployment type, end-user industry, and application, with Asia-Pacific expected to show the highest growth.

title: New Leveraged ETFs Let Traders Bet Big On Dell, Intel, Qualcomm
summary: GraniteShares has launched three new leveraged single-stock ETFs offering 2x amplified exposure to Dell Technologies (DELL), Intel (INTC), and Qualcomm (QCOM). These ETFs, the first of their kind for these specific tech names, allow investors to trade without margin accounts and target those bullish on the AI revolution. The firm also expanded its offerings in the options income space with its YieldBoost strategy, starting with GraniteShares YieldBoost TSLA (TSYY).

title: Career Connections
summary: The article details the Spring Business Career Fair hosted by the Rockwell Career Center, where over 1,600 Bauer students networked with prominent Houston employers. Recruiters from companies like Academy Sports + Outdoors, Dell Technologies, and H-E-B sought to fill employment opportunities across various business disciplines. Both students and employers benefited from the in-person interactions, fostering connections and allowing companies to assess the quality of students beyond resumes.

summary: The GraniteShares 2x Long DELL Daily ETF (DLLL) aims to deliver twice the daily price return of Dell Technologies Inc. stock, less fees and expenses. The article provides key financial metrics for DLLL, including its assets under management, expense ratio, and performance data over various periods. It also highlights the fund's active management style, its leveraged nature, and notes that it invests solely in cash.

title: Micron Redefines Performance for AI PCs, Gamers and Professionals
summary: Micron Technology announced the new Micron 4600 PCIe Gen5 NVMe SSD, designed to significantly boost performance for AI PCs, gamers, creators, and professionals. Leveraging Micron G9 TLC NAND, this SSD delivers sequential read speeds of 14.5 GB/s and write speeds of 12.0 GB/s, effectively halving AI model loading times compared to Gen4 SSDs and offering improved energy efficiency. The 4600 SSD is compatible with leading processors from AMD and Intel and integrates advanced security features.

title: Super Micro surges to be top performer in S&P 500 on hopes of big AI server deals
summary: Shares of Super Micro Computer Inc. (SMCI) soared, continuing a four-day trading streak and becoming the S&P 500's best performer this year. This surge is driven by investor hopes of significant artificial-intelligence server deals. Analysts speculate that Super Micro will greatly benefit from large AI server contracts, extrapolating from rumored deals involving rival server manufacturers Dell and HPE, though these deals remain unconfirmed by the companies themselves.

title: Cisco unveils software-driven manufacturing, partners with Rockwell Automation
summary: Cisco has launched software-defined manufacturing and partnered with Rockwell Automation to introduce a 'Digital Skills for Industry' skilling program in India. This initiative aims to address the significant skills gap in the Indian manufacturing sector by providing essential digital skills in areas like cybersecurity, IoT, AI, and automation. The program is crucial for India to achieve its goal of exporting $1 trillion worth of goods by 2030 and to maintain global competitiveness.

title: Earnings call transcript: Navitas Semiconductor Q4 2024 revenue miss hits stock
summary: Navitas Semiconductor reported its Q4 2024 earnings, with an EPS meeting expectations but revenue significantly missing forecasts ($18 million vs. $23.99 million). This shortfall led to a 16.95% after-hours stock drop. Despite this, the company maintains a strong cash position, anticipates a recovery starting in Q2 2025, and aims for positive EBITDA by 2026, driven by growth in data center, EV, and mobile markets.

title: Work-from-home warning as more bosses drag staff back into the office: ‘Domino effect’
summary: A "domino effect" is leading more Australian employers to mandate full-time office work, with nearly two in five requiring five days a week in the office. This trend, driven by companies like Amazon and Dell, is increasing the average required office days and reducing remote work flexibility. Despite some employee pushback and demands for higher salaries to compensate for commuting costs, employers are banking on improved office environments and career development to entice staff back.

title: Wipro Ventures secures $200 million in fresh funds, eyes bigger bets on startups
summary: Wipro Technologies has committed an additional $200 million to its venture capital arm, Wipro Ventures, marking its fourth round of fundraising. Wipro Ventures focuses on early- to growth-stage enterprise software startups, with total investments now approaching half a billion dollars across 37 startups and 12 exits over the past decade. This investment reinforces Wipro's strategy to foster innovation and create collaborative ecosystems between startups and large enterprises.

title: Top Distribution Execs Are Grappling With Financial Impact Of Tariffs
summary: Top executives from leading technology distributors like Ingram Micro, Arrow Electronics, TD Synnex, and D&H Distributing are preparing for the financial impact of new tariffs imposed by President Donald Trump. While they anticipate increased costs will be passed down to end-users, questions remain about price elasticity and potential delays in purchases. Distributors are leveraging past experiences, adjusting supply chains, and focusing on transparent communication with partners to navigate the evolving situation.

title: NetApp Stock Sinks After Data Storage Firm's Sales Miss Estimates
summary: NetApp (NTAP) stock declined after the company reported mixed fiscal third-quarter results. Earnings met expectations, but sales of $1.64 billion were below analyst estimates, and the company's guidance also fell short of Wall Street's consensus view.

title: NetApp misses Q3 expectations as deal slippage hits sales
summary: NetApp reported disappointing Q3 FY2025 results, missing revenue targets with a modest 2 percent year-over-year rise to $1.64 billion, attributed largely to deal slippage late in the quarter. CEO George Kurian acknowledged the "below our standards" performance and outlined steps to improve execution, despite experiencing growth in public cloud services and securing over 100 AI and data lake modernization deals. The company anticipates a rebound in Q4, but analysts maintain a cautious outlook.

title: Dell class-action lawsuit settlement giving Canadians $85 to $3,000 | National
summary: A $2.1 million class-action settlement against Dell has been approved in Nova Scotia, allowing thousands of eligible Canadians to claim between $85 and $3,000. The lawsuit stemmed from data thefts in 2017 by Dell's former service provider, which led to customers being targeted by tech support scams. Eligible individuals, who were notified by Dell or Verita Global, can receive a base payment of $85 without proof of loss, or up to $3,000 with proof of economic damages such as fraudulent charges or repair costs.

title: MWC 2025: Intel Showcases Foundational Network Infrastructure with Xeon 6
summary: At MWC 2025, Intel presented its Xeon 6 system-on-chip (SoC) with integrated AI, designed to modernize network infrastructure. The new SoC offers significant improvements in RAN capacity, performance-per-watt, and AI RAN performance compared to previous generations, aiming to reduce total cost of ownership for operators. Intel is collaborating with over 50 partners and customers, including Vodafone, AT&T, Samsung, and Verizon, to accelerate the adoption of these AI-powered networking solutions and drive sustainability in the telecommunications industry.

title: HPE stock crashes as CEO weighs in on job cuts, earnings miss, and DOGE
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) announced significant job cuts and a material warning on full-year profits after its stock crashed due to disappointing server operating margins. CEO Antonio Neri attributed the miss to aggressive discounting and inventory devaluation, impacting HPE's financial outlook and leading analysts to reduce profit estimates. Rival Dell also saw a stock dip following HPE's announcement.

title: Hewlett Packard Enterprise shares tumble as US tariffs hurt forecast
summary: Shares of Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) fell 13% after the company announced that its annual profit forecast would be negatively impacted by new U.S. tariffs, particularly affecting its server business. HPE plans to mitigate these impacts through supply-chain measures and pricing actions, but analysts expect a difficult second and third quarter before a potential recovery. The tariffs, including additional duties on Chinese goods, are also affecting rivals Dell and Super Micro Computer, leading to job cuts at HPE due to increased competition.

title: Statutory Profit Doesn't Reflect How Good NetApp's (NASDAQ:NTAP) Earnings Are
summary: Despite initially underwhelming investors, NetApp's recent earnings are better than statutory profit suggests due to strong free cash flow conversion. The company recorded a negative accrual ratio of -0.22, indicating its free cash flow of US$1.3 billion significantly understated its statutory profit of US$1.14 billion. This robust cash conversion, coupled with a 22% annual EPS growth over three years, suggests strong underlying earnings potential.

title: Ciena isn’t too worried about tariffs right now
summary: Ciena is currently unconcerned about potential tariffs impacting its business, especially given its diversified supply chain across Canada, Mexico, India, and Thailand, though it acknowledges the fluid situation. The company is optimistic about continued growth driven by AI-related bandwidth demand, with CEO Gary Smith highlighting strong performance from service providers and cloud segments. Ciena's leadership believes the issues of excess equipment inventory are largely resolved, setting the stage for optical fiber's comeback.

title: Climb Global Solutions Moving To Cloud Marketplace ‘To Make It As Easy As Possible’ For MSPs
summary: Climb Global Solutions is launching a new cloud marketplace called Climb Expedition to streamline transactions for its MSP partners, aiming for increased speed and efficiency. This strategic move follows a record-breaking 2024 for Climb, with significant growth in net sales and income, and is intended to help partners scale their businesses by simplifying access to emerging technologies and managing recurring revenue models. CEO Dale Foster emphasizes that the marketplace fills a portfolio gap rather than mimicking competitors, and the company will continue to focus on organic growth through vendor recruiting and operational efficiency in 2025.

title: Dell’s new RTX Pro AI PC boasts an ‘unlimited turbo’ mode
summary: Dell has announced its new "Pro Max" lineup of workstation desktops and laptops, featuring Nvidia's RTX Pro Blackwell GPUs and an innovative "unlimited turbo" mode for sustained high performance in its Tower T2 desktop. This series targets AI developers and power users, offering advanced cooling, large displays, and significant memory and storage capacities, including options with tandem OLED screens and modular USB-C ports. The range will include both Intel Core Ultra Series 2 and AMD Threadripper CPUs, with some models shipping in March and others in July.

title: Storage vendors rally behind Nvidia at GTC 2025
summary: At GTC 2025, twelve major storage vendors, including Cloudian, Cohesity, Dell, HPE, Hitachi Vantara, NetApp, VAST Data, and WEKA, announced support for Nvidia's AI stack, integrating their products with Nvidia's Blackwell Ultra GPUs and AI Data Platform. These integrations focus on providing high-performance storage, data management, and AI infrastructure solutions to accelerate AI training and inferencing workloads for enterprise customers. The announcements feature new reference architectures, software enhancements for AI data processing, and optimized systems leveraging Nvidia's GPU, networking, and software technologies.

title: 13 New AI-Focused Technologies For Nvidia AI Ecosystem At Nvidia GTC 2025
summary: The Nvidia GTC 2025 conference showcased 13 new AI-focused technologies from various vendors, highlighting advances in AI and related systems beyond Nvidia's own GPU and DPU developments. Companies like Dell, HPE, Supermicro, Anaconda, and DeepTempo introduced a range of hardware, software, and services designed to enhance AI processing, deployment, and cybersecurity. These offerings aim to simplify AI integration, improve security, and accelerate AI innovation across different industries from enterprise to cloud-based solutions.

title: How today's AI revolution relies on Equinix's data centers
summary: Equinix is positioning itself as a crucial enabler of the AI revolution by providing purpose-built data centers capable of supporting advanced AI workloads, including those powered by Nvidia's latest GPUs. Adam Berlew, Equinix's CMO, highlights the company's global infrastructure and partnerships as key to democratizing AI, offering "AI factories" as a managed service to various enterprises. This infrastructure allows companies like Dell and HP to deploy their AI initiatives within Equinix's facilities, overcoming the challenges of power and cooling for intensive AI operations.

title: Data Center Hardware Market Size, Share & Trends Chart by 2033
summary: The global data center hardware market was valued at USD 74.6 billion in 2024 and is projected to reach USD 157.5 billion by 2033, growing at a CAGR of 8.7%. This growth is driven by the increasing demand for high-speed, low-latency processing, the rise of AI and machine learning, and the expansion of hyperscale data centers. North America currently leads the market, while Asia Pacific is expected to be the fastest-growing region.

title: Alibaba’s Tsai Warns of ‘Bubble’ in AI Data Center Buildout
summary: Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. Chairman Joe Tsai has warned of a potential bubble in AI data center construction, suggesting that the current buildout pace might exceed the actual demand for AI services. He noted that many projects seem to be initiated without clear customers, indicating an indiscriminate rush by tech firms and investors. Tsai shared these concerns at the HSBC Global Investment Summit in Hong Kong.

title: PTC Brings AI-Powered PLM to Hannover Messe 2025 with Windchill AI
summary: PTC is set to preview its AI-powered Windchill AI product lifecycle management (PLM) assistant at Hannover Messe 2025, alongside Codebeamer AI and ServiceMax AI. This initiative demonstrates PTC's commitment to advancing agentic AI across the product digital thread, aiming to enhance engineers' efficiency by leveraging product data for informed decision-making and task automation. The preview will include the Document Vault AI agent, designed to extract critical product information from Windchill-stored documents.

title: Lumentum introduces optical circuit switch for AI data centers
summary: Lumentum Holdings Inc. is introducing its R300 optical circuit switch (OCS) with 300x300 port capability, designed to improve scalability and performance in AI data centers. The product, currently being sampled by hyperscale customers, is expected to be generally available in the second half of 2025 and aims to provide power savings of 65% or more for large-scale GPU deployments. Industry experts anticipate increased importance for OCS technologies as hyperscale operators prioritize scaling AI systems.

title: PTC to Showcase Windchill AI at Hannover Messe 2025: Stock to Gain?
summary: PTC is set to showcase its new Windchill AI at Hannover Messe 2025, highlighting its advancements in AI-powered product lifecycle management (PLM) and generative design. The company will also feature collaborations with tech giants like Microsoft, Dell, and Accenture, emphasizing the role of AI in streamlining product development and manufacturing. Despite strong business momentum and strategic partnerships, PTC recently lowered its 2025 sales forecast due to forex headwinds and tough sales conditions.

title: Palladyne AI CEO Benjamin Wolff buys $14,145 in common stock
summary: Benjamin Wolff, CEO of Palladyne AI Corp. (NASDAQ: PDYN), recently acquired 2,500 shares of the company's common stock for approximately $14,145, increasing his direct ownership to over 1.3 million shares. This purchase reflects his confidence in Palladyne AI despite a recent 14% stock decline, supported by the company's strong gross profit margins of 55.2% and recent positive financial performance, including increased revenues and a reduction in operating expenses for 2024. The company has also launched new software, secured a contract with the Air Force, and expanded its board of directors.

title: Here's How Much You Would Have Made Owning Dell Technologies Stock In The Last 5 Years
summary: Dell Technologies (NYSE: DELL) has significantly outperformed the market over the last five years, with an average annual return of 24.83%. title: Trump’s new tariffs could be devastating for telco supply chains say analysts
summary: New reciprocal tariffs announced by President Trump are expected to significantly raise the price of telecom equipment, impacting supply chains and potentially forcing carriers to increase service prices. Analysts from Dell'Oro, IDC, and neXt Curve agree that the tariffs will lead to higher equipment costs and lower demand, with OEMs like Dell and HPE likely to be hit harder than Ericsson and Nokia due to their manufacturing locations. These tariffs extend beyond China to countries like Vietnam, India, and Malaysia, complicating diversification efforts and potentially affecting consumer prices for devices and telecom services.

title: Labaton Keller Sucharow Mourns the Passing of Chairman Christopher J. Keller
summary: Labaton Keller Sucharow LLP announced the passing of its Chairman, Christopher J. Keller, a visionary leader who significantly impacted the firm and the legal community. Chris Keller was instrumental in securing landmark victories for investors and consumers, including record-setting settlements in major securities and privacy cases. His colleagues remember him as a titan in the plaintiffs' bar, a champion of justice, and a fearless advocate whose contributions will leave a lasting legacy.

title: C Spire Business Attains Titanium Partner Status with Dell Technologies
summary: C Spire Business has achieved Titanium partner status with Dell Technologies, signifying deep expertise in Dell's offerings and enabling them to provide tailored strategic guidance and streamline IT procurement for clients. This elite status grants C Spire Business access to Dell's full portfolio at competitive prices and dedicated executive support. The achievement highlights C Spire Business's significant investment in time, training, product expertise, and aligned resources with Dell Technologies.

summary: This article identifies the 25 hottest AI companies for data center and edge computing in 2025, according to CRN. These alliances aim to ensure applications remain operational, protected, and secure by leveraging Absolute's Application Resilience and Persistence Technology to counter security gaps and software vulnerabilities. Partners benefit from enhanced security offerings, continuous protection against cyber threats, and seamless integration capabilities.
