summary: Activist investor Elliott Investment Management has acquired a stake worth over $1.5 billion in Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE), making it one of the company's top five shareholders. Elliott aims to engage with HPE to boost its value, likely through divestiture, reform, or reorganization, citing HPE's recent stock decline and ongoing challenges like tariffs, weak server margins, and difficulties with the Juniper acquisition. Given Elliott's history with other tech companies, this could lead to significant strategic changes and increased pressure on HPE's management.

title: Kyndryl wants to help enterprises leverage their most sensitive data in AI workloads
summary: Kyndryl Holdings Inc. has launched new AI Private Cloud services to help enterprises securely deploy AI applications using sensitive data. These services are designed for regulated industries like financial services and healthcare, addressing concerns about data privacy and regulatory compliance. Kyndryl leverages its expertise and partnerships, including with Nvidia, to provide comprehensive consulting, design, and deployment assistance for private cloud AI environments.

title: Simplify Barrier Income ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NYSEARCA:SBAR)
summary: This article provides detailed information on the Simplify Barrier Income ETF (NYSEARCA:SBAR), including its current price, holdings, key statistics, and an overview of its investment strategy. The ETF, managed by Simplify Asset Management Inc., invests in a mix of public equity and fixed income markets globally, utilizing derivatives to create its portfolio. It also offers recent news headlines related to SBAR and addresses frequently asked questions about the fund's performance and ownership.

title: Prominent Hedge Fund Acquires Huge Stake in HPE: Is It a Buy Now?
summary: Elliott Management has acquired a significant 7.5% stake in Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE), causing HPE's shares to rise by over 5%. This move injects activist investor pressure into HPE, which has seen strong performance in 2024 but a decline in 2025 due to profitability concerns despite growth in its server segment. While Elliott's past actions with Dell suggest potential for positive change, their overall five-year performance has lagged the S&P 500, leading analysts to advise a cautious "wait-and-see" approach for HPE investors.

title: Elliott Management takes a stake in Hewlett Packard Enterprise. How the firm may create value
summary: Elliott Management has taken a $1.5 billion stake in Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE), seeing significant undervaluation due to poor execution and a pending acquisition of Juniper Networks. The activist investor believes its involvement, potentially through board representation, can restore shareholder confidence and ensure value creation regardless of the Juniper acquisition's outcome, leveraging its strong track record in tech activism. HPE's diverse portfolio, especially its high-multiple networking business, positions it favorably against competitors and potential geopolitical headwinds.

title: Command the data center lifecycle: maximize flexibility, uptime, and infrastructure investments in the AI expansion boom
summary: This article discusses the challenges and opportunities for data center operators in maximizing flexibility, uptime, and infrastructure investments amidst the AI-driven expansion boom. It emphasizes the need for strategic partnerships to navigate the complex landscape of technology, supply chain, and sustainability goals. Flex highlights its integrated approach, leveraging advanced manufacturing, power, cooling solutions, and lifecycle services, including the use of analytics and AI, to optimize data center operations and investments.

title: One radio, 12 dozers, zero downtime – Newmont plans private 5G at 14 mines on four continents
summary: Newmont Corporation is rapidly expanding its use of private 5G networks across 14 mines on four continents after successful trials at its Cadia mine in Australia. A single Ericsson 5G radio demonstrated the ability to connect 12 dozers at a construction site with zero downtime and improved uplink throughput, proving significantly more reliable than the previously unstable Wi-Fi. This initiative aims to enhance autonomous operations, connect various mining equipment, and accelerate the company's digital transformation vision for smart mining.

title: Veeam debuts data resilience maturity model to help enterprises reduce cyber risk exposure
summary: Veeam Software Group GmbH unveiled its Data Resilience Maturity Model (DRMM) at VeeamON, a framework designed to help organizations objectively assess and improve their data resilience posture against cyberattacks. The model emerged from a collaboration with industry experts including MIT and McKinsey, highlighting a significant disconnect between CIOs' perception of their organizations' data resilience and the reality, with 70% believing they are adequately protected, while the DRMM found fewer than 10% actually had acceptable levels. The DRMM categorizes maturity across four horizons—Basic, Intermediate, Advanced, and Best-in-class—and aims to help companies close this gap, improve recovery times, and reduce financial losses from outages.

title: NTNX Stock Price, News & Analysis
summary: Nutanix, Inc. (NASDAQ: NTNX) operates in the information sector as a software publisher focused on hybrid multicloud computing, offering a unified, cloud software platform for running applications and AI and managing data across different environments. The company emphasizes its Nutanix Cloud Platform (NCP) designed to support hybrid multicloud infrastructure, including deployments in core data centers, at the edge, and in public clouds with a consistent operating model. Nutanix highlights its subscription-based business model, global customer base, and focus on AI, security, and compliance, with offerings like Nutanix Kubernetes Platform (NKP) and Nutanix Enterprise AI (NAI).

title: Dell Technologies Stock Price, News & Analysis
summary: This article provides an in-depth analysis of Dell Technologies (DELL), covering its business focus, financial structure, AI infrastructure initiatives, and segment reporting. It highlights Dell's role as a top AI infrastructure provider, its diverse product portfolio including client systems and enterprise solutions, and its commitment to shareholder returns through dividends and strategic debt management. The article consolidates information from recent company announcements and SEC filings to offer a comprehensive overview of Dell's operations and market position.

title: Equinix beats expectations in first quarter and raises full year outlook
summary: Equinix Inc. surpassed revenue and earnings expectations in its first fiscal quarter of 2025, reporting adjusted earnings of $3.50 per share on revenue of $2.25 billion. The company also increased its full-year revenue outlook, now expecting $9.175 billion to $9.275 billion, a 5% to 6% rise from previous forecasts. This positive performance is attributed to robust demand for digital infrastructure, strategic enhancements to its platforms, and a new collaboration with Dell Technologies to bolster AI infrastructure offerings.

title: First Solar stock plunges as CEO says Trump tariffs pose ‘significant economic headwind’
summary: First Solar's stock plunged after its CEO, Mark Widmar, stated that President Donald Trump's tariffs were a "significant economic headwind" and more disruptive than anticipated. The company cut its full-year guidance, with estimated earnings and revenue significantly lower than previous forecasts. First Solar, the largest publicly traded solar module manufacturer in the U.S., indicated that these tariffs could lead to reduced or idled production at some of its international facilities.

title: Pivotal Stock Spikes on VMware Acquisition Overture
summary: Pivotal's stock surged after news broke that VMware is proposing to acquire all of Pivotal's outstanding Class A shares for $15 each, a price identical to its IPO offer. This acquisition by its sister company, both operating under Dell Technologies, aims to streamline operations and reduce market confusion, despite some analyst concerns regarding financial implications and governance. The deal signifies VMware's continued acquisition strategy, following recent purchases of AI-based firms Uhana and Bitfusion.

title: Building a greener future with sustainable innovation
summary: This article discusses the growing importance of sustainability for CEOs, particularly in light of regulations like the Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive (CSRD). It highlights how organizations need comprehensive strategies for efficient operations and carbon emission reduction, especially given the surging data volumes driven by AI. NetApp's approach to sustainability is detailed, focusing on aligning product development with sustainable outcomes, reducing greenhouse gas emissions through SBTi-approved targets, using renewable energy, and implementing sustainable product design and lifecycle management.

title: Unleash Scalability and Performance with Dell PowerFlex and Nutanix
summary: Dell Technologies and Nutanix have made their joint solution, Dell PowerFlex with Nutanix Cloud Platform, generally available. This offering combines Dell's high-performance storage with Nutanix's software-defined architecture, aiming to enhance IT modernization, operational efficiency, and simplify management for mission-critical environments. The collaboration marks PowerFlex as the first external storage integrated with Nutanix Cloud Platform, offering expanded support for Nutanix AHV.

title: Nutanix Next CEO Keynote: 6 Big Remarks On Google, AWS, Cisco And Pure Storage
summary: During the Nutanix Next 2025 keynote, CEO Rajiv Ramaswami announced significant partnerships and innovations with Google Cloud, AWS, Cisco, Dell Technologies, and Pure Storage. Key highlights include Nutanix's availability on Google Cloud, the new FlashStack solution with Cisco and Pure Storage, and the general availability of Nutanix's cloud platform integrated with Dell PowerFlex. Ramaswami also detailed the refactoring of Nutanix AOS to be fully cloud-native on Amazon's EKS, emphasizing multi-cloud portability and resilience.

title: Nutanix marries cloud-native infra with Pure Storage and agentic AI
summary: Nutanix is advancing its platform to be cloud-native and hypervisor-independent, announcing new integrations and an Enterprise AI initiative. Key developments include cloud-native AOS, general availability of Dell PowerFlex support, and integration with Pure Storage FlashArray and FlashStack. These moves aim to provide a versatile software platform capable of running diverse workloads anywhere, including supporting agentic AI with Nvidia.

title: Flex: We'll pass tariff costs along
summary: Flex, an Austin-based manufacturer, anticipates passing tariff costs along to its customers without significant disruption to its global operations. The company, which has 30 large factories worldwide, manufactures a variety of products for other large companies and is also seeing strong revenue from data centers.

title: These 10 tech stocks could benefit most from softening U.S.-China tariffs
summary: The article discusses how an easing trade war between the U.S. and China, particularly a temporary agreement on tariffs, could significantly benefit certain tech stocks. Companies like Apple and Dell are identified as potential major beneficiaries due to their supply-chain exposure to tariffs. The temporary agreement involves a 90-day pause on tariffs and a 115% reduction in reciprocal tariffs from both sides.

title: Venture X Business vs. Amex Business Platinum: Which business card is right for you?
summary: This article compares the Capital One Venture X Business and the Amex Business Platinum cards, detailing their annual fees, welcome offers, earning rates, benefits, and lounge access. It aims to help small-business owners decide which premium card best suits their needs, noting that while the Amex Business Platinum has more benefits and a higher fee, the Venture X Business offers competitive perks at a lower cost.

title: HPE adds Morpheus Data to KVM hypervisor for enterprises
summary: Hewlett-Packard Enterprise has enhanced its KVM hypervisor with technology and capabilities acquired from Morpheus Data, aiming to compete with leading virtualization platforms. The new offerings, including HPE Morpheus VM Essentials and HPE Morpheus Enterprise, provide hybrid cloud management tools and third-party integrations, available through HPE GreenLake or as standalone software. This strategic move positions HPE as a significant player in the virtualization software market, especially following Broadcom's acquisition of VMware.

title: The Stuff I Use And Where It Comes From
summary: The author reflects on the origins of everyday items due to anticipated tariffs, finding that many products she uses, from her computer to clothing, are "Made in China" or other foreign countries. She notes the difficulty in consistently finding "Made in the USA" goods, concluding that while individuals should seek out American-made products, the local newspaper remains a consistently domestically produced item.

title: NuScale expects 77-MWe design approval in July, first SMR order by December
summary: NuScale Power anticipates approval for its uprated 77-MWe small modular reactor (SMR) design from the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission in July 2025, with CEO John Hopkins suggesting a potential first SMR operating power plant delivery by 2030 if a deal materializes soon. The company is actively pursuing firm contracts with potential customers, including data centers and industrial operators, moving away from Memoranda of Understanding. NuScale expects to achieve cash-flow positive status within the first year of a committed power plant project, although supply chain tightness for nuclear components could initially limit the number of orders.

title: Broadcom vs. Marvell: Which Semiconductor Stock Has Greater Upside?
summary: This article compares Broadcom (AVGO) and Marvell Technology (MRVL), two key semiconductor providers in the AI data center market, to determine which stock offers greater potential upside. While both companies benefit from the growing semiconductor market, Broadcom is favored due to its expanding AI portfolio, strong partner base, and favorable Zacks Rank compared to Marvell's near-term challenges with excess chip inventories and export restrictions. The analysis highlights Broadcom's leadership in custom AI accelerators and its expected revenue growth in AI and semiconductor segments, positioning it as a better buy despite both being considered overvalued.

title: NVIDIA Launches AI-First DGX Personal Computing Systems With Global Computer Makers
summary: NVIDIA has announced new DGX Spark and DGX Station personal AI supercomputers, built by leading manufacturers like Acer, ASUS, Dell Technologies, GIGABYTE, HP, Lenovo, and MSI. These systems are designed to offer unprecedented AI performance and efficiency for developers, researchers, and data scientists, addressing the growing demand for robust AI computing solutions that can handle agentic AI systems. Powered by NVIDIA Grace Blackwell platforms, they enable prototyping, fine-tuning, and inference of models from desktop to data center, with DGX Spark available in July and DGX Station later this year.

title: NVIDIA Powers Humanoid Robot Industry With Cloud-to-Robot Computing Platforms for Physical AI
summary: NVIDIA has announced significant advancements in cloud-to-robot computing platforms for physical AI, introducing NVIDIA Isaac GR00T N1.5, an updated foundation model for humanoid reasoning, and Isaac GR00T-Dreams, a blueprint for generating synthetic motion data. These innovations aim to accelerate humanoid robot development by enabling faster training and adaptation to new environments, with major robot manufacturers already adopting NVIDIA's Isaac platform technologies. The company emphasizes its role in providing comprehensive building blocks for the robotics development journey, from AI brains to simulation environments.

title: Intel Gaudi 3 Expands Availability to Drive AI Innovation at Scale
summary: Dell's AI platform, featuring Intel Gaudi 3 AI accelerators, is now available to help enterprises with AI adoption. This end-to-end solution combines Intel Gaudi 3 hardware with an open-source software stack and Dell's infrastructure, offering high performance, flexibility, and cost-efficiency for various AI workloads. The platform boasts competitive generative AI price-performance, a scalable architecture, and an optimized environment for developers and IT teams.

title: Arkansas getting a new Google data center
summary: Google is planning to build a new $10 billion data center in West Memphis, Arkansas, code-named Project Pyramid. This will be the third large data center in Arkansas, following earlier announcements for Conway and Little Rock. The facility will be powered by Entergy Arkansas and is expected to employ around 300 workers.

title: 4 stocks to watch on Thursday: COST, DELL, KSS and ELF
summary: This article highlights four stocks—Costco (COST), Dell (DELL), Kohl's (KSS), and ELF Beauty (ELF)—to monitor on Thursday. The market is experiencing a positive sentiment boost following a trade court ruling that struck down most of President Trump's tariffs. Costco (COST) is specifically mentioned as a stock to watch.

title: Vi Business, HPE to Deliver Managed Networking Services in India
summary: Vi Business, the enterprise arm of Vodafone Idea, has partnered with Hewlett-Packard Enterprise (HPE) to offer managed networking services to businesses across India. This collaboration will leverage HPE Aruba Networking equipment and Vi's connectivity, integrating AI and ML for enhanced user experience and aiming to co-develop solutions for private data centers. The initiative seeks to improve operational efficiency and drive digital transformation for enterprises in both private and public sectors.

title: Will Hewlett Packard Enterprise Stock Surge or Sink After Its Upcoming Earnings?
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) is slated to release earnings on June 3, 2025, with analysts projecting $0.33 per share on $7.5 billion in sales. Historically, HPE stock has shown a positive one-day return in 63% of instances after earnings, with a median gain of 3.4%. The article analyzes historical post-earnings stock performance, correlations between short-term and medium-term returns, and peer influence to help traders strategize.

title: Alta Global Group (MMA) Short Interest & Short Float | Updated Mar 2026
summary: This article provides an updated analysis of Alta Global Group's (MMA) short interest as of October 15, 2025. It details a short interest of 935,800 shares, representing an 8.50% decrease from the prior report, and calculates a short interest ratio of 0.2 days to cover. The report also includes historical short interest data and answers frequently asked questions about short selling metrics for MMA.

title: HPE Uses AI To Drive The Business, Which Is Increasingly AI
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) is undergoing a restructuring, internally dubbed "Catalyst," focused on cost reduction, operational efficiency, portfolio optimization, and leveraging AI to streamline operations and enhance customer engagement. The company reported a significant operating loss in Q2 F2025 due to a $1.4 billion impairment related to Nvidia Hopper GPUs, as customers shifted demand to Blackwell GPUs, yet AI system revenues showed a strong increase, especially from enterprises. HPE aims to achieve a leaner, faster, and more competitive organization by using AI to improve efficiency across its business and expects operating income to rise by Q4 F2025.

title: Dell Technologies vs. HPQ: Which PC Stock Has More Growth Potential?
summary: This article compares Dell Technologies (DELL) and HP (HPQ) as investments in the growing PC market, which is projected to reach $344.13 billion by 2030. It highlights DELL's strong AI infrastructure, enterprise focus, and robust earnings outlook, especially with its AI PCs and partnerships with NVIDIA and Microsoft. HPQ is also expanding its AI PC portfolio but faces risks from its heavy reliance on Chinese manufacturing amid potential tariff escalations.

title: Lenovo Vs. HP Vs. Dell PC Sales Showdown In 2025 So Far
summary: The article details the 2025 PC sales performance of Lenovo, HP, and Dell, highlighting a PC refresh driven by commercial buyers ahead of the Windows 11 migration and potential tariff impacts. While IDC projects overall market growth, consumer sales remain challenged compared to strong commercial demand. The three companies show varying revenue and market share, with projections for continued PC growth in the coming quarters.

title: North America Consumer Electronics Market Size, Share, 2033
summary: The North America Consumer Electronics Market was valued at USD 245.11 billion in 2024 and is projected to reach USD 369.62 billion by 2033, growing at a CAGR of 4.67% from 2025 to 2033. Key drivers include rising disposable incomes, urbanization, and advancements in 5G and IoT technologies, while challenges involve supply chain disruptions, rapid technological obsolescence, and intense competition from budget brands. Smartphones dominate product categories, and retail stores remain the primary distribution channel, though e-commerce is rapidly expanding.

title: Nvidia makes big play for Europe with infrastructure deals
summary: Nvidia is significantly expanding its presence in Europe through new partnerships and infrastructure deals, focusing on building AI factories and sovereign AI capabilities. CEO Jensen Huang announced collaborations with European firms and governments, including a major deal with French startup Mistral, to deploy its GPUs for AI cloud services and establish tech centers for research and development. This strategic move aims to embed Nvidia's technology into national AI infrastructures and unlock new markets, particularly as the company faces export restrictions in China.

title: Here are Thursday's biggest analyst calls: Nvidia, Amazon, Palantir, Dell, SoFi, Oracle, BJ's, Lockheed Martin & more
summary: This article compiles major analyst calls on Wall Street for Thursday, featuring various company ratings and price targets. Key companies include Nvidia, Amazon, Palantir, Dell, SoFi, Oracle, BJ's, and Lockheed Martin, with analysts discussing valuations, growth prospects, and specific company initiatives. The reports highlight themes such as AI innovation, strong fundamentals, and market positioning.

title: Cisco Mission Statement | Vision | Core Values | Strategy (2025 Analysis)
summary: This article analyzes Cisco's mission statement, corporate social responsibility goals, and core values, providing a comprehensive look at the company's strategic direction for 2025. It details Cisco's commitment to shaping the future of work for all, embracing global reach, ensuring security, and promoting an inclusive future through environmental sustainability and community impact. The article also touches upon key company information, its products, services, and market position.

title: Diskover gets $7.5M from Snowflake, NetApp and others to facilitate unstructured data discovery for AI
summary: Diskover Data Inc. secured $7.5 million in seed funding from investors including Snowflake and NetApp to enhance its unstructured data discovery platform for AI. The startup also announced its first acquisition of CloudSoda Inc. and strategic partnerships aiming to help enterprises make their vast amounts of unstructured data usable, searchable, and secure for AI models. This funding will facilitate the expansion of Diskover's go-to-market and engineering teams.

title: Broadcom Delivers the Modern Private Cloud with VMware Cloud Foundation 9.0
summary: Broadcom has announced the general availability of VMware Cloud Foundation (VCF) 9.0, a platform designed for the modern private cloud. This new version aims to accelerate innovation, control cloud costs, and enhance sovereignty and security for customers across data centers, edge, and managed cloud infrastructure. VCF 9.0 introduces a new architecture with a unified interface for operations, frictionless consumption, and a unified platform supporting traditional, cloud-native, and AI applications.

title: Technology is making travel even easier this summer
summary: American Airlines is enhancing the summer travel experience through new technology, including a redesigned mobile app offering real-time updates and seamless one*world partner check-ins. The airline is also testing a generative AI-powered chat assistant for flight rebookings during disruptions and new technology at hubs to proactively identify and save connecting flights for customers. These innovations aim to make travel more convenient, reliable, and user-friendly.

title: Is It Time To Consider Buying Hewlett Packard Enterprise Company (NYSE:HPE)?
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (NYSE:HPE) has seen significant price movement recently and appears fairly valued according to Simply Wall St's model, trading about 10.66% above its intrinsic value. Despite this, the company's future earnings are projected to increase by 85%, indicating strong growth potential. Investors are advised to consider other factors like financial strength and market volatility, as the current valuation suggests it may not be the optimal time to buy, unless the price drops.

title: Dell Technologies President of Global Sales & Customer Operations William Scannell Sells 100% Of Holding
summary: William Scannell, Dell Technologies' President of Global Sales & Customer Operations, recently sold his entire holding of US$11 million worth of stock at US$118 per share. This substantial sale follows a US$1.2 billion insider sale by CEO Michael Dell within the last year. While insiders retain significant ownership (42%), the lack of insider buying and recent large sales raise concerns about the company's stock performance.

title: Digital Realty picks HPE Private Cloud to manage data center operations
summary: Digital Realty has selected HPE's Private Cloud Business Edition to manage its global data center operations across more than 300 facilities in 25+ countries. This move aims to simplify management, enhance security and resilience, and handle massive data volumes efficiently. The partnership highlights Digital Realty's trust in HPE's private cloud technologies, with the colocation firm also utilizing HPE's Alletra Storage MP B10000 servers for data storage.

title: Finally, Seagate will ship a single HDD with a capacity of 40TB
summary: Seagate has begun limited shipments of 40TB HDDs, utilizing thermally assisted magnetic recording (HAMR) technology and Mozaic 3+ with an areal density of 3TB per platter. The company plans to expand to Mozaic 4 for 40TB HDDs in the first half of 2026, targeting data centers. Seagate aims to achieve 100TB+ HDDs by 2030, addressing the surging demand for data center storage driven by AI.

title: Intel and Lenovo Set a New Standard for AI-Powered PC Experiences
summary: Intel and Lenovo collaborated extensively over several years to develop the new Lenovo Aura Edition line of premium AI PCs, which launched in September 2024. This partnership focused on redefining the premium laptop experience through extensive user feedback, co-engineering breakthroughs in memory performance and thermal management, and a global team effort. These AI PCs feature innovations like Smart Share, high refresh rate displays, and efficient AI software, with future evolutions planned based on Intel's Panther Lake CPUs.

title: Hewlett Packard Enterprise Company (HPE) Is Going To Be More Than An NVIDIA Assembler, Says Jim Cramer
summary: Jim Cramer discussed Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE), suggesting it could evolve beyond merely assembling NVIDIA components, especially following its approved acquisition of Juniper Networks. He also critiqued HPE's latest financial quarter, noting it outperformed lowered expectations but still lagged behind Dell Technologies in his view. Cramer remains cautious on HPE until significant business improvement or activist investor intervention occurs.

title: I built a brand new gaming PC from the ground up with Corsair's iCUE Link: a simplified, smart and just a little frustrating all-in-one system
summary: The author built a new gaming PC using Corsair's iCUE Link system, which aims to simplify component connectivity and control. While the system offers benefits like reduced cable clutter for some components and unified software control, the author found it somewhat frustrating due to thick proprietary cables, challenges with routing, and the need for additional standard cables for certain components. Despite its drawbacks and high cost, the system delivered excellent performance in the new all-AMD build.

title: About Us
summary: VivoPower aims to be the leading independent digital real estate and infrastructure partner, focusing on sovereign nations and hyperscalers. The company is a Certified B Corporation, committed to balancing profit with positive social and environmental impact. VivoPower's core values include strategic partnerships, efficient use of energy for computational assets, and solving infrastructure challenges in critical areas like AI data centers and sustainable energy.

title: Insight CEO: ‘We Are Pivoting To Become An AI-First Solutions Integrator’
summary: Insight Enterprises, led by CEO Joyce Mullen, is transforming into an "AI-first solutions integrator" to help clients navigate the rapidly evolving AI landscape. The company emphasizes continuous learning for its employees, ethical AI implementation, and delivering measurable results quickly for its primarily mid-market clients, actively seeking acquisitions to enhance its AI capabilities. With a focus on responsible AI, Insight aims to bridge the gap between AI hype and practical business value through strategies like the Radius AI assessment and specialized managed services.

title: Riding the Earnings Wave: 10 Stocks Backed by Upward Revisions
summary: This article identifies 10 well-known stocks that are receiving perfect scores from Validea’s Earnings Revision model, indicating strong upward revisions in analyst earnings forecasts. The strategy, based on Wayne Thorp's research, suggests that positive earnings revisions are often underappreciated by the market, creating investment opportunities. These companies span various sectors and are highlighted as potential outperformers due to improving fundamentals and rising expectations.

title: AI infrastructure reshapes capital markets - RAISE Summit
summary: The RAISE Summit highlighted how AI infrastructure is fundamentally transforming capital markets, making it a crucial factor in company scaling, competition, and IPO readiness. Investors are increasingly scrutinizing how companies integrate AI for innovation and long-term operational resilience, shifting the focus from mere growth to sustainable profitability. The event, featuring insights from major tech players and financial institutions, outlined 12 key ways AI infrastructure is redefining IPO processes, investor priorities, and enterprise growth strategies across all sectors.

title: Is It Too Late To Consider Buying NetApp, Inc. (NASDAQ:NTAP)?
summary: NetApp (NASDAQ:NTAP) appears to be fairly priced according to Simply Wall St's valuation model, trading about 14% below its intrinsic value. While its positive outlook and expected 19% profit growth over the next couple of years are encouraging, the stock's volatility suggests potential future buying opportunities. Investors are advised to consider additional factors like management track record and balance sheet strength before making a decision.

title: With 41% ownership in Dell Technologies Inc. (NYSE:DELL), insiders continue to have the largest holding even though they have sold shares recently
summary: Insiders, particularly CEO Michael Dell, hold the largest stake (41%) in Dell Technologies Inc., despite recent share sales, indicating a strong vested interest in the company's growth. Institutional investors also hold a significant share, representing a respectable portion of the company's registry. The top three shareholders collectively control over half of Dell Technologies, giving them considerable influence over company decisions.

title: Dell Technologies (DELL) director sells $751k in class C common stock
summary: A director at Dell Technologies (DELL), Egon Durban, sold Class C Common Stock totaling $751,621 on July 17, 2025. These sales occurred at $123.50 per share, involving shares sold through Silver Lake Group and entities associated with Durban. Dell's stock is trading near its Fair Value, and the company has shown a 14% return over the past six months, with recent analyst price target increases and ongoing scrutiny over federal contracts.

title: Dell Technologies’ Silver Lake sells $21.8 million in shares
summary: Silver Lake, a significant owner of Dell Technologies (NASDAQ: DELL), sold $21.8 million worth of Class C Common Stock on July 15, 2025, with prices ranging from $125.9 to $127.56 per share. The company has also seen recent conversions of Class B to Class C shares by Silver Lake Partners and its affiliates. Analyst Mizuho raised its price target for Dell to $150 based on AI server wins, while KeyBanc initiated coverage with a Sector Weight rating due to potential gross margin pressures from AI growth.

title: Corning staves off antitrust fine as EU regulators accept concessions to mobile phone makers
summary: The EU antitrust regulators have accepted concessions from glass maker Corning, averting a potential fine. Corning agreed to waive exclusive dealing clauses with mobile phone makers and glass processing companies and to scrap purchasing clauses, ending an eight-month investigation. This ensures that original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) are not required to buy more than 50% of their demand from Corning, a measure valid for nine years.

title: Consumer Electronics Market Trends, Growth Forecast & Opportunities 2025-2035
summary: The global Consumer Electronics Market is projected to grow from USD 750.5 billion in 2024 to USD 1594.2 billion by 2035, driven by technological advancements, rising consumer disposable income, and the proliferation of connected devices. Key trends include the rise of smart homes, wearable tech, and eco-friendly electronics, while challenges involve rapid innovation cycles and supply chain disruptions. North America is a dominant market due to high purchasing power and early tech adoption, presenting numerous opportunities in emerging economies and sustainable product development.

title: U.S. Computer Peripheral Market Size, and Growth Report, 2032
summary: The U.S. computer peripheral market is projected to grow from USD 12.5 billion in 2024 to USD 94.3 billion by 2032, exhibiting an 8.3% CAGR from 2025. This growth is driven by increasing demand for advanced computing devices, the rise of remote and hybrid work models, and technological innovations like wireless connectivity and ergonomic designs. Wireless peripherals and input devices are key segments, with online distribution channels also seeing significant expansion.

title: CDW cuts more workers in fourth round of layoffs over 2 years
summary: Computer reseller CDW recently laid off approximately 300 workers in its fourth round of cutbacks in just over two years, bringing the total estimated job reductions to around 1,500. This frequent workforce realignment has raised concerns about employee morale and the company's ability to retain and attract talent, despite the company's first-quarter sales and profit showing increases from the prior year. Analyst Keith Housum suggests these layoffs are primarily for skill-set realignment rather than a broad workforce reduction.

title: Canada's leading tech startup Cohere strikes alliance with BCE to sell AI tools
summary: Canadian AI startup Cohere and telecommunications giant BCE have formed an alliance to sell made-in-Canada AI tools, including large language models and an agentic AI platform, to Canadian businesses and governments. This partnership aims to bolster the country's "sovereign AI" capabilities by leveraging Bell Canada's AI data centers and fiber network to provide secure and private AI solutions. Cohere will also receive significant funding from Ottawa's Sovereign AI Compute Strategy and is expanding its data center presence in Ontario.

title: Intel to spin off networking business in cost-cutting gambit
summary: Intel plans to spin off its Network and Edge Group (NEX) into an independent entity, focusing on core CPU and AI initiatives. This move is part of the company's broader cost-cutting and streamlining strategy under CEO Lip-Bu Tan, similar to previous actions with its Altera and Mobileye divisions. While analysts see it as aligning with Intel's goal to play to its core strengths, some express concern that detaching networking from an "AI-first" strategy might be shortsighted given AI's reliance on robust networking infrastructure.

title: CBRE Arranges Two Major Leases for iDC Logistics in Southern California, Totaling Over 1.1 Million Square Feet
summary: CBRE has facilitated two major lease agreements for iDC Logistics in Southern California, encompassing over 1.1 million square feet of industrial space. These leases include an 844,311 square-foot Class A industrial facility in San Bernardino and a 260,000 square-foot facility in the City of Industry, which will serve as a manufacturing hub for consumer electronics. These expansions support iDC Logistics' national growth strategy and commitment to U.S. manufacturing.

title: Top 10: CSCOs in North America
summary: Supply Chain Digital has compiled a list of the Top 10 Chief Supply Chain Officers (CSCOs) in North America, highlighting their crucial role in managing efficient and cost-effective flow of goods and services. The article features prominent CSCOs from major companies like Nike, PepsiCo, and Colgate-Palmolive, detailing their responsibilities, career trajectories, and contributions to supply chain innovation, sustainability, and risk management. These executives are recognized for their leadership in navigating complex logistics, implementing new technologies, and fostering strong supplier relationships.

title: Lenovo Retains Top Spot in Gartner® Asia/Pacific Supply Chain Top 10 for Fourth Year
summary: Lenovo has once again secured the No.1 position in the Gartner® Asia/Pacific Supply Chain Top 10 for 2025, marking its fourth consecutive year at the top. The company also improved its global ranking, moving up to No.8 in Gartner’s Global Supply Chain Top 25. This recognition highlights Lenovo's commitment to AI innovation, operational excellence, ESG leadership, and its strategic "Lenovo Powers Lenovo" approach to supply chain management.

title: Introducing Commerce, the New Parent Brand of BigCommerce,
summary: BigCommerce Holdings, Inc. has rebranded to Commerce.com, Inc. (Commerce), unifying its subsidiaries BigCommerce, Feedonomics, and Makeswift to focus on an AI-driven future in agentic commerce. The company's stock will now trade under the ticker symbol "CMRC" on Nasdaq. Commerce aims to empower merchants with intelligent, composable tools and data infrastructure to thrive in an era where AI agents assist consumer purchasing decisions.

title: Channel Focus: All you need to know about HPE's partner program
summary: HPE's partner program is undergoing significant changes to support enterprise efficiency and productivity, particularly in AI and hybrid cloud environments. The company has unified 11 separate partner programs under the "HPE Partner Ready Vantage" umbrella, categorizing partners by capability (Sell, Service, Build) instead of traditional tiers. This restructuring aims to capitalize on market opportunities in AI, edge computing, and infrastructure transformation, with HPE estimating substantial channel opportunities in generative AI alone.

title: SNIA Announces Storage.AI
summary: SNIA, a global non-profit organization specializing in data technologies, has launched Storage.AI, an open standards project aimed at creating efficient data services for AI workloads. This initiative brings together industry leaders such as AMD, Cisco, Dell, IBM, Intel, and others to address critical challenges like latency, power, and cost in AI data pipelines through vendor-neutral solutions. Storage.AI seeks to develop an open ecosystem that optimizes performance and cost-effectiveness for AI, leveraging SNIA's extensive experience in industry standards development.

title: The Technology Behind General Motors’ MES 4.0
summary: General Motors is implementing MES 4.0, a manufacturing execution system, to achieve its goals of launching 30+ electric vehicles by 2035 and becoming carbon neutral by 2040. This involves bridging the gap between operational technology (OT) and information technology (IT) through model-based systems engineering, virtual design, and MES Ops. GM's long-standing relationship with GE Vernova is crucial to this initiative, leveraging their HMI/SCADA and industrial data historian platforms.

title: AMD Reports Second Quarter 2025 Financial Results
summary: AMD announced strong financial results for the second quarter of 2025, with record revenue of $7.7 billion, up 32% year-over-year. The company's growth was driven by record server and PC processor sales, despite a significant impact from U.S. export controls on its MI308 data center GPU products. AMD also provided an optimistic outlook for the third quarter of 2025, expecting revenue of approximately $8.7 billion.

title: Earnings call transcript: TSS Inc Q2 2025 results show strong revenue growth
summary: TSS Inc. reported a significant 262% revenue increase in Q2 2025, reaching $44 million, driven by its Procurement Services segment, despite a flat EPS of $0.06 year-over-year. The company's stock experienced an 18.85% premarket decline following the earnings release. TSS reiterated optimism for the full year, raising its Adjusted EBITDA outlook to at least 75% growth compared to 2024, bolstered by its new, fully operational Georgetown, Texas facility designed for high-performance computing systems.

title: Banzai International, Inc. Schedules Conference Call to Discuss Q2 2025 Financial Results and Future Initiatives
summary: Banzai International, Inc. (NASDAQ: BNZI) will host a conference call on Thursday, August 14, 2025, at 4:30 p.m. Eastern Time to discuss its second-quarter financial results for the period ending June 30, 2025, and review ongoing initiatives. Founder & CEO Joe Davy and CFO Dean Ditto will lead the call, which will include a Q&A session and a presentation available on the company’s investor relations website, aiming to provide updates on its AI-enabled marketing and sales solutions. The company serves over 90,000 customers and emphasizes its commitment to innovation and future growth through its forward-looking statements.

title: Broadcom Inc. Announces VMware Explore 2025 Featuring Keynote by CEO Hock Tan and Global Tour Plans
summary: Broadcom Inc. announced VMware Explore 2025, scheduled for August 25-28 in Las Vegas, will feature over 400 technical sessions and a keynote address by CEO Hock Tan focusing on private cloud and AI innovation. The event will include networking opportunities, sponsor interactions, and training. Following the main event, the "Explore on Tour" initiative will bring curated sessions to cities across Asia-Pacific, Japan, and Europe.

title: Super Micro Stock Falls 23%: Falling Knife Or Buying Opportunity?
summary: Super Micro Computer stock (NASDAQ: SMCI) has dropped significantly after a tough Q4 2025 earnings report, missing estimates and showing margin contraction despite its strong position in AI servers. The company faces increasing competition from Dell, HPE, and Lenovo, which are impacting its revenue growth and profitability. Furthermore, SMCI has twice slashed its full-year revenue guidance and consistently missed earnings estimates, raising concerns about its valuation and corporate governance.

title: Nayax Announces Strategic Partnership With Leading Global EVSE Provider Autel Energy to Deliver Embedded Payment Solutions
summary: Nayax Ltd., a global commerce enablement and payments platform, has announced a strategic partnership with Autel Energy, a leading electric vehicle (EV) charging technology provider. This collaboration aims to integrate Nayax's payment solutions directly into an estimated 100,000 Autel EV chargers across North America and Europe by the end of 2026. The partnership seeks to simplify payments for EV drivers and optimize operations for charging site owners amidst the growing demand for EV charging infrastructure.

title: Ethiraj Purushothaman Joins Perrigo as Global Head of Data, Analytics and AI
summary: Ethiraj Purushothaman has been appointed as Vice President, Global Head of Data, Analytics & AI at Perrigo Company plc. In this role, he will oversee Perrigo’s global data strategy, analytics, and AI initiatives, aiming to integrate these functions into the company's business strategy. Purushothaman brings extensive experience from previous leadership roles at major companies like Best Buy, The Coca-Cola Company, and Dell Technologies, where he developed and scaled advanced analytics programs.

title: Dell Faces Tracking Devices and Security Breach Challenges
summary: Dell Technologies Inc. Class C (NYSE: DELL) stock is down 4.19% amidst market uncertainties. The company faces challenges from chip tracking regulations, a security breach affecting investor confidence, and the need to revitalize its Client Solutions Group under new leadership. The article discusses Dell's financial overview, market position, and the impact of these events on its stock stability.

title: Dell Technologies Inc. Cl C stock falls Wednesday, underperforms market
summary: Dell Technologies Inc. Cl C (DELL) stock experienced a drop of 4.97% on Wednesday, closing at $128.48. This decline occurred during a mixed trading session for the broader market, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average rising and the S&P 500 Index falling. This marks the sixth consecutive day of losses for Dell's stock.

title: Purpose-built Backup Appliance (PBBA) Market | Global Market Analysis Report - 2035
summary: The Purpose-built Backup Appliance (PBBA) market is projected to grow from USD 14.4 billion in 2025 to USD 40.2 billion by 2035, exhibiting a CAGR of 10.8%. Cloud-based PBBAs are expected to dominate with a 54.2% market share, while PBBA hardware will lead the component segment at 47.8%. North America is identified as the most lucrative region, driven by digitization, cloud computing, and increasing data privacy concerns, with the US anticipated to have the highest market value.

title: Commvault expands HyperScale portfolio with new edge and high-performance options
summary: Commvault Systems Inc. has introduced two new additions to its HyperScale portfolio, HyperScale Edge and HyperScale Flex, to enhance cyber resilience and data protection for enterprises. HyperScale Edge targets remote sites and SMBs with limited IT resources, offering localized protection and ransomware resilience.
